title: Claims Management Processing Officer
description: Job Overview The Claims Management – Processor, evaluate more complex data entry, clerical, and accounting tasks involved in the preparation and processing of patient insurance claims. Supervises releasing team with regards to productivity and efficiency. What You Do Responsibilities will include, but are not limited to, the following: AI and Coding Standards: Familiarity with AI tools that can automate the coding process, ensuring accuracy and efficiency in applying standards like ICD9, ICD10, CPT, HCPCs, Dental codes, ATC, etc. Understanding of AI algorithms that can analyze and correlate different types of codes (e.g., ICD to CPT correlation) to improve coding practices. AI in Fraud Detection: Knowledge of AI systems that can detect patterns of medical claims fraud and abuse practices, such as contra-indication, unbundling, and double billing. Ability to leverage machine learning models to identify anomalies and predict fraudulent activities. AI in Auditing: Experience with AI-driven auditing tools that can streamline operations processes and provide real-time insights. Ability to implement AI solutions that enhance the accuracy and efficiency of audits. AI in Training and Coaching: Use of AI platforms to develop personalized training programs for operations staff, improving their understanding of coding and fraud detection. Ability to coach staff on using AI tools effectively in their daily tasks. AI and Analytical Skills: Proficiency in using AI analytics tools to enhance problem-solving capabilities and derive actionable insights from complex data sets. AI in Time Management: Familiarity with AI scheduling and task management tools that optimize time management and workflow efficiency. AI in Professional Interaction: Ability to use AI communication tools to enhance interactions with external parties, ensuring clarity and professionalism. AI in Confidentiality and Teamwork: Understanding of AI systems that ensure data security and confidentiality while facilitating collaboration in a matrix environment. AI in Technical Skills: Proficiency in AI-enhanced MS Office applications that improve productivity and data analysis capabilities. AI READINESS Work effectively in an environment shaped by artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning, data, analytics and cloud-based tools, using insights responsibly with our standards of data governance, security and ethical use. What You Bring To be successful in this position you will need to have the following skills/ experience: AI in Documentation: Familiarity with AI tools that automate filing and documentation processes, improving accuracy and efficiency. Ability to leverage AI for organizing and retrieving documents quickly. AI in Project Management: Use of AI-driven project management tools that provide predictive analytics for revenue forecasting and resource utilization. Understanding of AI systems that enhance project tracking and reporting capabilities. AI in Healthcare Industry: Awareness of AI applications in healthcare for improving operational processes and patient data management. Ability to integrate AI solutions to streamline workflows in TPAs, insurance companies, hospitals, and medical centers. AI in Technical Skills: Proficiency in AI-enhanced MS Office applications that offer advanced data analysis and visualization features. Use of AI tools for efficient internet navigation and research. How We Hire Agency Statement Allianz Partners does not accept unsolicited CV’s or approaches from agencies. We only work with partners on our approved supplier list, under contract. Any unsolicited submission will not be considered Key Benefits/What We Offer Allianz Partners is a world leader in B2B2C insurance and assistance, offering global solutions that span international health and life, travel insurance, automotive and assistance. Customer driven, our innovative experts are redefining insurance services by delivering future-ready, high-tech high-touch products and solutions that go beyond traditional insurance. Our products are embedded seamlessly into our partners’ businesses or sold directly to customers, and are available through four commercial brands: Allianz Assistance, Allianz Automotive, Allianz Travel and Allianz Care. At Allianz Partners, Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming service delivery and innovation. By leveraging Business Intelligence and Data Analytics, we enhance strategic decision-making and customer experiences. Our commitment to Ethical AI ensures responsible deployment, maintaining data integrity and trust. Conversational AI improves communication, while Emerging Technologies and Generative AI drive industry leadership and new opportunities. With robust Information Security Management, we protect sensitive information, ensuring compliance and security. Embrace the future of AI with Allianz Partners, where possibilities become realities. 92007 | Customer Services & Claims | Professional | Non-Executive | Allianz Partners | Full-Time | Permanent Allianz Group is one of the most trusted insurance and asset management companies in the world. title: Data Engineer [F/M/X]
description: Job Description You will join the Data Engineering team at the heart of our evolution, contributing to the design, development, and operation of scalable data platforms supporting multiple business domains such as finance, recruitment/HR, and operational data. This is a hands-on, delivery-focused role, working closely with Data Analysts, Data Scientists, and business stakeholders to provide reliable, analytics-ready data in a structured Agile environment. Your missions Design, build, and maintain production-grade data pipelines on Microsoft Azure Own end-to-end ELT/ETL workflows, from raw ingestion to curated analytics layers Implement data processing using Azure Data Factory, Azure Synapse Analytics, Dataflows, and PySpark notebooks Contribute to data ingestion and processing involving Azure Cosmos DB, Azure Functions, and Logic Apps for event-driven or API-based workflows Support the adoption and usage of Microsoft Fabric (Lakehouse, Data Engineering workloads) as a platform evolution Design scalable and well-structured data models Deliver trusted datasets for Data Analysts (dashboards, KPIs, reporting) and Data Scientists (feature engineering, machine learning pipelines) Optimize SQL queries and Spark workloads for performance and cost efficiency Implement data quality checks, validation rules, and monitoring Apply best practices in Python and PySpark development, SQL performance tuning, and maintainable code Participate in code reviews and contribute to engineering standards Work in an Agile environment (Scrum) and use Azure DevOps for backlog management, pull requests, CI/CD, and release pipelines Enable automated validation, controlled deployments, and reliable promotion of data engineering assets across development, test, and production environments Support and mentor junior engineers and interns on data engineering practices Collaborate closely with analytics and data science teams to understand and anticipate data needs Contribute to architecture discussions and continuous platform improvement Promote best practices and knowledge sharing within the team Your profile Experience in data engineering (3–5 years) Proficiency with Azure data platforms, especially Azure Synapse Analytics and Azure Data Factory Experience with SQL (complex queries, optimization) Experience with Python for data processing and pipeline logic Experience delivering production data pipelines Experience working in Agile teams with structured delivery processes Experience using Azure DevOps (backlogs, pull requests, CI/CD pipelines) Familiarity with Microsoft Fabric (Lakehouse, Data Engineering workloads) is a plus Exposure to Azure Cosmos DB and Azure Functions in data ingestion or integration contexts is a plus You demonstrate strong problem-solving and communication skills You are pragmatic, delivery-oriented, and business-aware English fluent. Mastering generative AI such as ChatGPT for improving day-to-day productivity What We Offer An international community bringing together 110+ different nationalities An environment where trust has a central place: 70% of our key leaders started their careers at the first level of responsibilities A robust training system with our internal Academy and 250+ available modules A vibrant workplace that frequently gathers for internal events (afterworks, team buildings, etc.) At Mantu, sustainability is part of everything we do. You’ll have the opportunity to turn your ideas into action and make a tangible impact. Every day, our teams bring our ESG commitments to life, from reducing our footprint to driving positive change within our communities. Through our WeCare Together program, you’ll be empowered to design and lead projects that create real social or environmental impact, with the company’s full support. Mantu is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to promoting diversity within the workforce and creating an inclusive working environment. For this purpose, we welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, beliefs, age, marital status, disability, or other characteristics. Who are we? Mantu is an independent international consulting player, founded in 2007. Bringing together expert and complementary brands, Mantu stands out for the breadth of its spectrum, responding to all business transformation challenges. Its activities are divided into four practices: Leadership & Advocacy, Technology, Brand Strategy & Experiences and Total Talent Management. A wide range of skills, all serving a single mission: connecting and powering companies with leading teams and technology to succeed faster and sustainably. From its headquarters in Geneva, Switzerland, Mantu relies on a community of 12,000 talented people in more than 60 countries on 5 continents and has a turnover of 1billion euros.

title: Buyer
description: Job Description Job Title: Buyer Location : Tunis Reporting to: Purchasing Manager Overview of Role: As a Buyer you assume the overall responsibility of purchasing activities of promotional goods for our sites in Czech Republic, Tijuana Mexico, Tennessee, and any other location as needed. You will be responsible for ensuring all purchase orders (mostly Vendors based in China) are processed in a timely manner, monitor forecasts, supply capacity with vendors and assess the optimum time to place orders in consideration of forecasts, lead times and production schedules. You will manage stock ordering to ensure we carry sufficient stock to avoid stock outs whilst avoiding carrying too much stock to manage cashflow. This role requires you to be a hands-on contributor tackling tactical and more complex issues where analysis of situations and data are required. Primary Responsibilities Manage the availability of inventory to your assigned SKUs to satisfy production and customer needs. Closely monitor forecasted material needs Quick effective response to urgent requirements Schedule and Track orders and ensure timely delivery to all plants. Responsible for maintaining data integrity of safety stocks, purchase order dates, lead times, and other key targets related to the purchasing process. Responsible for minimizing air shipments through accurate order management. Manage safety stock levels and process of recommendation to forecasting teams for required adjustments. Identify, assess, and communicate supply disruption risks to your supervisor and key stakeholders. Manages relationships with Accounts Payable to ensure accurate payment to suppliers and reduce/minimize discrepancies in invoices against purchase orders Identifies and often implements plans for continuous operational improvement within the department. Manage the procurement process from requisition to delivery, ensuring compliance with company policies and procedures. Identify opportunities for cost savings and process improvements. Other duties as assigned. Required skills and knowledge: Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain, Business, Engineering, or related field. 2+ years’ experience in procurement, materials planning, or supply chain operations. Proven track record in process improvement, preferably with Lean Six Sigma Green Belt or similar certification. Strong understanding of automation tools and processes and data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau). Exposure to AI/ML concepts in forecasting, demand planning, or procurement is highly desirable. ERP experience (SAP, Oracle, NetSuite, or equivalent). Analytical mindset with excellent Excel and data handling skills. Strong communication, problem-solving, and change management skills. Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal Digitally curious: eager to learn and apply new technologies. Process-driven: structured thinker with attention to efficiency and accuracy Collaborative: able to engage cross-functionally with IT, finance, marketing, NPI and operations. Innovative: continuously seeks smarter, faster, and better ways to work. Accountable: delivers results and maintains a high standard of operational discipline. Self-Driven: Ability to work independently and manage multiple projects simultaneous Our values At Pens.com, we are guided by a set of core values that define our culture and approach to hiring: Customers-First: We are committed to delivering exceptional customer experiences, both internally and externally. By understanding and fulfilling customer needs, we create impactful solutions that leave a lasting impression. Continuous Improvement: We believe in the power of growth and evolution. Through open communication and a culture of innovation, we continuously strive to enhance our business and adapt to changing landscapes. Goal-Oriented: We set clear objectives, remain disciplined in execution, and hold ourselves accountable for achieving measurable goals. Our focus is on driving results and making meaningful progress. Integrity: We prioritize ethics in everything we do, ensuring that our actions are grounded in integrity and responsibility towards our planet, products, and people. People-Centric: We value and respect every member of our team, fostering an environment where diversity is celebrated, contributions are recognized, and growth is encouraged. We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation - no applicant will be penalized as a result of such a request. About Pens.com Pens.com's personalized promotional products are created for and inspired by small businesses. With 56 years of experience and serving 22 countries worldwide, Pens.com offers MORE value, savings and time to customers through expert guidance on a broad range of personalized promotional products, including writing instruments, stationery, drinkware, bags, gifts, and trade show accessories. Pens.com operates via a network of more than 10 facilities across North America, Europe, Africa, and Asia. To learn more, visit: www.PENS.com Pens.com is a National Pen and Cimpress brand (Nasdaq: CMPR).

title: Head of BOS Integrated Services Hub 1
description: About This Opportunity Ericsson is looking for a Head of Integration Services Hub1 within Service Line (SL) Business and Operations Systems (BOS) as a key leadership role part of BOS Delivery Excellence, responsible for leading the service excellence. The role focuses on services business pre-sales and overlooks integrated delivery models setup including System Integration, Application Development & Maintenance , and IT Managed Services. This role reports to the Head of SL BOS Delivery Excellence for MEMA and will be part of BOS Delivery Excellence Leadership team. What You Will Do: Lead BOS service excellence, managing team of senior services professionals. Accountable for services engagement and pre-sales including target topline and profitability. Responsible for aligning with Solution Area on Services GTM and drive services offering build up , presales and delivery engagements for the EMEA region. Secure service delivery readiness through competence planning inline with business and portfolio evolution. Senior People Manager of own unit and a senior Leader across matrixed organization extending to solution line professionals in other CU BOS, BOS solution lines and SD/SDU units. Adopt, position and expand integrated service delivery offerings; with focus on customer value realization, delivery efficiency, and adapting to different commercial models. Champion delivery efficiency initiatives in collaboration with program management, CU BOS SL and SD/SDU. Set Individual Performance and Development goals for members with regular check-ins. Responsible for team dimensioning; and Cost Center Responsible to deliver over-Absorption target and required Utilization/Billability. Drive a Knowledge Sharing and Asset building culture across their community. Instill a culture of add-on sales and customer value maximization. What You Will Bring: 12+ years of experience in telecom industry with leadership track record in professional services including system integration, application development & maintenance, and IT managed services. Deep understanding of BOS portfolio including products and services. University degree in ICT/Engineering. A graduate degree such as an MBA is a plus. Strong People Manager track record leading Senior Team members and leading Leaders. Deep Telecom knowledge including understanding of Telecom networks, OSS & BSS components and familiarity with TmForum (eTOM, SID, TAM) Advanced experience with IT Systems and Integration OSS and BSS full stack integrations and surrounding ecosystems API management & microservice architecture Cloud-Native & Platform transformation Data analytics, AI/ML applications in service operations In depth knowledge of different SDLC methodologies including Agile, Waterfall and DevOps approaches. Proven experience with MS IT and ADM related frameworks – ITIL, COBIT & SAFe Demonstrated experience leading cross-functional highly matrixed and globally distributed teams. Recommended certifications (external): ITIL, Lean Six Sigma, PMP Recommended certifications (internal): MSCOO, ICT PD JS8 GIAP certification Strong consultative, presentation, and communication skills Proven track record to deliver results within a time sensitive and high-pressure environment. Highly proficient collaboration skills Solid ability to communicate effectively at executive levels. In-depth knowledge of running Organizational and Solution Level Governance. Fluent in English; additional languages are a plus. Note: There are a total of 4 Head of Integration Services Hub vacancies with the same responsibility and skills requirements. Ericsson South Africa recruits in line with its Employment Equity plan and will therefore give preference to suitably qualified candidates who are members of designated groups. The chance to use your skills and imagination to push the boundaries of what´s possible. To build solutions never seen before to some of the world’s toughest problems. You´ll be challenged, but you won’t be alone. You´ll be joining a team of diverse innovators, all driven to go beyond the status quo to craft what comes next. What happens once you apply? Click Here to find all you need to know about what our typical hiring process looks like. Encouraging a diverse and inclusive organization is core to our values at Ericsson, that's why we champion it in everything we do. We truly believe that by collaborating with people with different experiences we drive innovation, which is essential for our future growth. We encourage people from all backgrounds to apply and realize their full potential as part of our Ericsson team. Ericsson is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. learn more. Req ID: 776664

title: Data gouvernance team lead
description: With its beginnings in a family run pharmacy in Correggio, Italy in the 1920s, Recordati is now a global pharmaceutical company, listed on the Italian stock exchange, with over 4,500 employees and turnover of over Euro 2bn. We are a group of like-minded, passionate individuals who go to extraordinary lengths for our patients, customers, partners, investors and the people across the globe who we serve. We develop and commercialise medicines to serve people living with common diseases, as well as those living with some of the rarest, in around 150 countries. At Recordati, our mantra is simple. We’ve always believed that health, and the opportunity to live life to the fullest, is a right, not a privilege. Whether that is for common diseases or the rarest – we want to give people the opportunity to be the best version of themselves. This drive will never stop. Together, we will always be reimagining tomorrow – with new ideas, new technologies and new innovations to fight diseases. Recordati. Unlocking the full potential of life. Mission principale L’Ingénieur IT spécialisé en Data Science et Intelligence Artificielle est chargé de piloter les projets de transformation digitale de l’entreprise en s’appuyant sur l’exploitation des données, l’intelligence artificielle et les technologies numériques. Il encadre une équipe de spécialistes IT et coordonne les projets visant à améliorer la performance, l'efficacité opérationnelle et la prise de décision grâce à des solutions digitales innovantes. Il agit comme référent technique et stratégique pour les initiatives data et IA au sein de l’organisation. Responsabilités principales Pilotage des projets de transformation digitale Identifier les opportunités de digitalisation des processus métiers. Piloter les projets de transformation digitale de l’entreprise. Définir les feuilles de route technologiques. Assurer la planification, le suivi et la réussite des projets IT. Garantir le respect des délais, budgets et objectifs. Management de l’équipe IT Data & IA Encadrer et coordonner une équipe de spécialistes IT. Répartir les projets et les responsabilités au sein de l’équipe. Développer les compétences techniques de l’équipe. Assurer un suivi régulier des performances et de l’avancement des projets. Favoriser une culture d’innovation et de collaboration. Développement de solutions Data Science & IA Superviser la conception et le développement de modèles : Machine Learning ; Intelligence artificielle ; Analyse prédictive Assurer la mise en production et le déploiement des solutions développées. Garantir la qualité et la fiabilité des modèles. Gouvernance et stratégie Data Participer à la définition de la stratégie Data de l’entreprise. Mettre en place des politiques de gouvernance des données. Garantir la sécurité et la conformité des systèmes de gestion des données. Support à la prise de décision Développer des outils analytiques avancés pour la direction. Fournir des analyses stratégiques basées sur les données. Contribuer à la mise en place d’une culture data-driven dans l’entreprise. Formation Diplôme d’Ingénieur Informatique avec un baground en (Data Science, Intelligence Artificielle, Mathématiques appliquées, Statistiques) Expérience Minimum 5 ans d’expérience en : Data Science / IA ; gestion de projets IT ; transformation digitale Expérience en management d’équipe IT souhaitée. Une expérience dans l’industrie pharmaceutique est un avantage. Compétences techniques Technologies Data & IA : Machine Learning ; Deep Learning ; Data Analytics ; Big Data Programmation : Python ; R ; SQL Outils analytiques : Power BI ; Tableau ; Data Visualization Architecture IT : Cloud Computing (AWS / Azure / GCP) ; Data Warehouse ; APIs ; DevOps / MLOps Compétences managériales Leadership d’équipes techniques Gestion de projets IT Conduite du changement Communication transverse Gestion des priorités Orientation résultats Langues Français : courant Anglais : professionnel At Recordati we believe in people! Inspired by our purpose - unlocking the full potential of life - we are committed to creating a diverse environment and cultivating a culture of inclusion. title: Junior Graphic & Motion Designer
description: Position Overview Join our creative team as a Junior Graphic & Motion Designer. In this role, you will primarily focus on graphic design and motion content creation for Questel and its brands/subsidiaries. You will support marketing campaigns, corporate communication, digital content, and events through strong visual storytelling and dynamic motion assets. You will collaborate closely with marketing, communication, and digital teams to create impactful and brand-consistent visual materials across multiple platforms. Key Responsibilities Graphic Design Design visual assets for websites, social media, presentations, print materials, events, and internal communications. Create digital marketing materials such as banners, visuals, logo adaptations, business cards, and promotional assets. Ensure visual consistency across all designs in line with brand guidelines. Support marketing campaigns with creative concepts and high-quality visual execution. Motion Design & Video Production Create motion graphics for social media, events, product launches, and marketing campaigns. Design animated visuals, transitions, branded intros/outros, and short promotional videos. Edit corporate and marketing videos using typography animation, sound, and visual effects. Ensure consistency of animation style with the brand identity. Digital Design Support Provide visual assets for web pages and landing pages. Collaborate closely with marketing, web, and communication teams. Communicate ideas clearly and incorporate feedback constructively. Respect deadlines and ensure high-quality deliverables. Continuous Improvement Stay up to date with motion design trends, digital content formats, and creative tools. Propose innovative visual approaches to enhance brand communication. Continuously improve production efficiency and design quality. Qualifications Degree in Graphic Design, Motion Design, Digital Media, or related field (or equivalent experience). At least 1 year of hands-on experience in graphic and/or motion design. Strong proficiency in Adobe Illustrator, Photoshop, Premiere Pro, After Effects, Canva & Figma. Strong understanding of branding, composition, and animation principles. Good communication skills in English and French. Creative, detail-oriented, organized, and comfortable working in a fast-paced environment.

title: Motion Graphics Designer
description: Job Description We are looking for a talented and detail-oriented Senior Motion Graphics Designer to join our creative team. The ideal candidate is a master of visual storytelling, able to bring concepts to life through animation, typography, and motion design. You will play a key role in shaping dynamic content across digital, social, and video platforms while ensuring consistency with brand identity and creative vision. title: Graphic Designer
description: Yassir est la super application leader dans le domaine des services à la demande, opérant de manière prédominante dans la région du Maghreb. Yassir a pour mission de révolutionner la manière dont les services quotidiens sont accessibles. Actuellement implantée dans plus de 45 villes à travers plusieurs pays tels que l'Algérie, le Maroc, la Tunisie et le Sénégal, notre présence s'étend aussi sur d'autres pays tels que la France et l'Afrique sub-saharienne. Au-delà de la simple prestation de services, notre vision est de créer un marché dynamique visant à satisfaire les besoins de nos utilisateurs tout en insufflant des valeurs sociales fondamentales. Responsabilités : Analyse des briefs marketing : Prendre en charge et analyser les briefs et demandes émanant du service marketing afin de les traduire en concepts visuels statiques pertinents. Création de concepts visuels : Concevoir des visuels créatifs en s’appuyant sur différents outils graphiques afin de transmettre efficacement les messages marketing et atteindre les objectifs de communication. Exécution des supports graphiques : Produire les supports visuels nécessaires aux campagnes marketing tout en respectant le brief, les exigences et les délais définis. title: Graphic Designer
description: Rattaché(e) au Responsable Studio Graphique, vous participez à la création, à la mise à jour et à l’évolution des documents techniques du Groupe Socomec : catalogues produits et services, notices, guides techniques et supports dédiés. Vous travaillez en étroite collaboration avec les équipes marketing produit, communication et commerciales afin de garantir une production graphique cohérente, qualitative et conforme à nos standards. Qualité documentaire Garantir la qualité linguistique des documents (orthographe, grammaire, typographie en français et en anglais). Vérifier la cohérence visuelle et technique des assets produits. Très bonne compréhension des règles de mise en page, typographie, couleurs et chartes graphiques. Aisance en français et en anglais pour assurer la cohérence linguistique des contenus. Les plus qui pourraient vous rendre unique Expérience préalable dans un rôle similaire au sein d’un studio graphique ou d’un service marketing. Connaissance des environnements industriels ou techniques. Sens du service, excellente communication et capacité à travailler avec différents métiers. Organisation, réactivité et respect des délais. Parce que votre profil ne se résume pas qu’à des compétences métier Curiosité créative et goût pour les solutions graphiques innovantes. Rigueur, précision et souci du détail.

description: Lieu : Centre Urbain Nord, Tunis Type de contrat : CDI / CIVP Entreprise : Lyceena Academy Démarrage : Dès que possible Horaires : 40h par semaine (hybride) Lyceena Academy est une plateforme éducative innovante dédiée aux lycéens Dans le cadre du développement de notre présence digitale, nous souhaitons renforcer notre identité visuelle sur les réseaux sociaux à travers des contenus créatifs, modernes et engageants. assets pour les campagnes saisonnières) Développer et maintenir l'identité visuelle de Lycéena (Respect et enrichissement de la charte graphique sur tous les supports digitaux) Concevoir des infographies et supports pédagogiques visuels (Contenus éducatifs, résumés visuels et capsules graphiques adaptés aux lycéens) Adapter les formats selon les plateformes (Déclinaisons pour Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, WhatsApp) Produire des supports visuels ponctuels ponctuel (Flyers, affiches et supports de communication selon les besoins) Assurer une veille active sur les tendances graphiques et digitales (Formats viraux, styles visuels émergents, pratiques des concurrents) Proposer et itérer en continu (Nouvelles idées de formats visuels et angles créatifs — en s'appuyant sur les et forte sensibilité visuelle Capacité à décliner une identité visuelle de façon cohérente sur plusieurs formats Autonomie, organisation et gestion de plusieurs projets en parallèle Rigueur et respect expérience minimum de 2ans en Design pour les réseaux sociaux Maitrise des outils Suite Abode, photoshop et Illustrator Excellente connaissance des codes de la GenZ : codes visuels TikTok, Instagram, Snapchat Portfolio obligatoire, au moins 5 exemples de visuels RS (posts, stories, carrousels)- variété de supports appréciée Bonus : Sensibilité au monde éducatif ou expérience dans les contenus title: Cybersecurity Analyst
description: Position Title: Cybersecurity Analyst Department: IT Services Reports To: IT Department Senior Manager Position Overview The Cybersecurity Analyst is responsible for protecting SMU’s information systems, networks, and digital assets. This role plays a critical part in ensuring the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of institutional data while supporting SMU’s academic and operational activities. The ideal candidate is a technically skilled professional with a strong sense of responsibility and ethics, capable of identifying risks, responding to incidents, and continuously improving SMU’s cybersecurity posture in line with international standards and best practices. Key Responsibilities Security Operations and Risk Management Ensure the security of systems, networks, applications, and databases. Identify, analyze, and remediate vulnerabilities across the IT environment. Implement and maintain information security policies and procedures (ISMS / PSSI). Monitor security events and manage incident response activities. Auditing, Testing, and Compliance Conduct security audits, vulnerability assessments, and penetration tests. Support compliance with cybersecurity frameworks and regulations (ISO 27001, GDPR, etc.). Maintain technical documentation, procedures, and security reports. Monitoring and Tool Management Operate and manage security tools such as SIEM, EDR, antivirus, and vulnerability scanners. Perform log analysis, event correlation, and reporting using FortiAnalyzer. Administer Fortinet solutions including FortiGate and FortiAnalyzer. User Awareness and Continuous Improvement Raise awareness among staff and students regarding cybersecurity best practices. Conduct technology and regulatory monitoring to recommend improvements. Contribute to strengthening SMU’s overall cybersecurity maturity. Profile And Qualifications Required Qualifications Bachelor’s, Master’s or Engineering degree in Computer Science, Networking, Cybersecurity, or related field. 1–3 years of experience in cybersecurity or IT security (motivated junior profiles with strong internships will be considered). Solid knowledge of network security (firewalls, VPN, IDS/IPS). Experience with Linux and Windows Server environments. Familiarity with application and database security. Hands-on experience with Fortinet solutions (FortiGate, FortiAnalyzer). Understanding of cryptography and identity/access management (IAM). Basic knowledge of cloud security (Azure, AWS, or equivalent). Fluent French and technical proficiency in English. Preferred Qualifications Experience with SIEM platforms, EDR solutions, and vulnerability scanners. Exposure to cloud security architectures. Professional certifications in cybersecurity (e.g., ISO 27001, Fortinet, CEH, Security+) are an asset. Personal Attributes Strong sense of confidentiality, integrity, and professional ethics. Analytical mindset with strong problem-solving abilities. Capacity to perform under pressure and manage crisis situations. Team-oriented with good communication skills. Autonomous, organized, and detail-oriented. To apply, please send your CV and cover letter to hr.development@smu.tn

title: SOC Analyst, Tunis - Remote
description: Our Team Cimpress is the world leader in mass customization. You’ve probably heard of our brands, including Vistaprint, WIRmachenDRUCK, Pixartprinting, and others. Our businesses can choose the solutions that work for them or assemble any custom combination they need. It makes it easier and faster to introduce new products, reach customers, and track orders. And this kind of innovation keeps customers coming back. Just last year, Cimpress generated $2.88B in revenue through customized print products, signage, apparel, packaging, and more. We encourage our engineers to think like an owner – to continue to act small as we grow. Every team definests roadmaps and uses the programming languages and technologies that suit them best. This helps us have a significant impact at the enterprise level while still feeling small and nimble. The Cimpress Security Team is a diverse team of technologists motivated to enable and empower our teams to move fast but securely. Our mission is to protect and secure Cimpress Business across the globe. What You Will Do Assess, triage, investigate and respond to alerts that originate from the cyber security product suite, e.g. SIEM, NGFWs, IDS/IPS, Anti-virus, Web Application Firewalls, EDR, etc. Recognize potential, successful, and unsuccessful intrusion attempts and compromise then address them in accordance to established SOC procedures. Carry out technical vulnerability assessments of IT systems to identifying potential vulnerabilities and make recommendations to control identified risks Respond rapidly and effectively to IT security incidents, including performing forensics for evidence gathering and preservation. Keep up to date with security trends, threats and control measures and apply them as needed throughout the organization. Assist with the preparation of reports, brief nags, and documentation. Contribute to the identify cation of process inefficiencies and continuous improvement of security monitoring, response, and controls. Create content to expand the monitoring scope and optimize operations as needed (rules, automation, etc.) Manage other activities that may arise through evolution and growth. Your Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or a related field. 1+ year experience in SOC operations and security event analysis or 3+ years of experience in IT security/network related field required. Cyber security certifications preferred Experience reviewing and correlating logs in a security capacity strongly preferred. Understanding intrusion tools and techniques and detection methods at both the network and host level. Knowledge of cloud security (AWS, Azure, GCP, etc.) required Knowledge of threat intelligence tools and methodologies required Experience with programming and scripting languages Understanding of main compliance standards and data regulation requirements strongly preferred Effective Communication both verbally and in writing. Strong analytical skills, attention to detail and critical thinking Approaching tasks with flexibility, proactivity and completing work to a high quality Collaborative and team player. Why You'll Love Working Here We encourage our engineers to think like owners – to continue to act small as we grow. Every team defines their own roadmaps, and uses the programming languages and technologies that suit them best. This helps us have a big impact at the enterprise level while still feeling small and nimble. We have a Remote-First operating model and culture, with team members in over 15 countries and 30 US states, offering autonomy and the flexibility to work from home. Cimpress also provides collaboration spaces for team members to work physically together. About Us Led by founder and CEO Robert Keane, Cimpress invests in and helps build customer-focused, entrepreneurial mass customization businesses. Cimpress Technology develops cutting-edge, best-in-world software that our mass customization businesses use to create personalized physical (and digital) products, for over 17 million global customers. Our businesses can choose the solutions that work for them, or assemble any custom combination they need. This makes it easier and faster to do things like introduce new products, reach customers and track orders. And this kind of innovation keeps customers coming back. Commitment to Diversity, Equity, & Inclusion Cimpress exists to help our customers live their dreams. Each dream is unique – and the Cimpress team needs to be as well. We believe in the unique contributions of everyone within a diverse global organization. We are collaborative, inclusive, and innovative. We strive to role model and live an inclusive culture of fairness, respect and belonging for all. And we work together to empower each other, creating a space in which each of us can spark our next great idea. description: Drive Vneuron’s information security strategy by building and leading an ISO 27001 compliant ISMS. For the fourth consecutive year, we’ve been honored in the RegTech100 , a global list of the most innovative companies in the industry. At Vneuron , we’re shaping the future of compliance and financial crime prevention. Join us in making a real impact in the world of RegTech. Our organizational structure is flat and collaborative, and our management is available and present. Key Responsibilities Design, implement, and maintain a comprehensive ISMS compliant with ISO 27001 Lead the organization toward ISO 27001 certification and audit readiness Identify, assess, and mitigate information security risks across all departments Strengthen data security, confidentiality, and incident response processes Integrate existing security controls (logical, technical, and physical) into the ISMS framework Conduct internal audits and continuous improvement initiatives Drive employee awareness and security training programs Monitor regulatory requirements (GDPR and others) and ensure compliance Profile & Requirements Engineering degree in Computer Science, Information Security, or related field 5–10 years of experience in information security, including senior leadership roles (CISO experience is a plus) Strong expertise in ISO 27001 frameworks and information security governance Solid understanding of GDPR and regulatory compliance Background in software security and secure development practices is a plus Excellent risk management and incident response capabilities Strong leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills Relevant certifications are a plus (CISSP, CISM, ISO 27001 Lead Implementer/Auditor, CISA, CRISC) Comfortable using AI tools for risk analysis, security monitoring, incident detection, compliance automation, reporting, and productivity improvement Key Objectives Achieve and maintain ISO 27001 certification Reduce organizational security risks and vulnerabilities Strengthen data protection and confidentiality Build a strong, security-aware culture across the company Establish continuous improvement in information security practices Your Next Steps If you have the ambition to stimulate your career with new challenges while capitalizing on your potential and your perpetual thirst for learning, this job is for you. title: Risk Analyst
description: Job Title: Risk Analyst Location: Remote (Worldwide) Job Summary: The Risk Analyst is responsible for identifying, assessing, and mitigating operational, financial, regulatory, and reputational risks that could impact organizational performance. This role analyzes data, processes, and control environments to detect vulnerabilities, evaluate exposure levels, and recommend effective risk mitigation strategies. Responsibilities Proactively monitor platform data to identify emerging risks, including payment fraud, account takeovers, spam campaigns, phishing scams, and synthetic identity networks. Use SQL, Python, or analytics to query large datasets, uncover attack vectors, and quantify the scale and impact of abusive behavior. Design and implement rules, models, and workflows to detect and prevent fraudulent or high-risk activity with minimal friction for legitimate users. Partner with Engineering, Product, Finance, and Legal teams to build risk controls into the product development lifecycle and ensure compliance with financial regulations. Serve as a key responder during active risks events (e.g., large-scale fraud spikes, payment processor threats). Make rapid decisions to contain losses while maintaining user trust. Build dashboards and reports to track key risk indicators (KRIs), communicate findings to leadership, and measure the effectiveness of mitigation strategies. Provide feedback on internal risk tools and collaborate with engineers to improve detection capabilities and automate manual reviews. Assist in evaluating and integrating third-party risk intelligence vendors (e.g., threat intelligence feeds, identify verification services). Requirements Minimum of 2 years of experience in Risk Analysis, Fraud Intelligence, Trust & Safety Analytics, or Financial Crime investigations within the tech, e-commerce, or fintech sectors. Strong SQL skills are essential. Experience with Python, R, or data visualization tools (Tableau, Looker). Ability to translate raw data into actionable insights. Comfortable working with large, messy datasets to find signals of abuse. Deep understanding of common attack vectors (e.g., chargeback fraud, promo abuse, botnets, deepfakes) and risk mitigation strategies (e.g., rule-based detection, machine learning models). Ability to make quick, high-stakes decisions during active incidents, balancing loss prevention with user experience. Strong ability to present complex technical findings to non-technical stakeholders, including executives and legal teams. Familiarity with global risk and compliance frameworks such as AML (Anti-Moey Laundering), KYC (Know Your Customer), GDPR, or PSD2 (Payment Services Directive). Proactive curiosity and willingness to dig deeper than surface-level metrics to uncover hidden abuse patterns. Ability to lead cross-functional initiatives and manage priorities in a fast-paced environment.

title: Security Engineer - SecOps & Cloud
description: Our Mission Swapcard is the leading AI-powered event platform designed to drive revenue growth and foster meaningful connections at in-person and hybrid events. This belief fuels our commitment to pioneering modern solutions that empower organizers to create engaging event experiences. Screening Interview with a recruiter from our people team A remote exercise to demonstrate and assess your skills Manager review with your future reporting manager Leadership review with one of our department leaders Reference check conducted by our people team Offer Swapcard's Values 🧠 Curious : We ask questions, try new things and take risks. Innovation thrives when curiosity drives. 🗝️ Value-Driven: At Swapcard, we focus on making each decision count by prioritising outcomes that create meaningful value for our customers, team members, and partners. 💚 Human : At Swapcard, being human means fostering empathy, openness, and diversity to create a caring and collaborative community. We’re driven by a strong team spirit and a shared goal of building meaningful connections—both through our product and within our team. ✊ Resilient : We embrace challenges with optimism, creativity, and adaptability, constantly seeking innovative solutions and opportunities for growth. 👑 Ownership: At Swapcard, we take responsibility and are accountable for our actions, driving success through initiative, trust, and accountability. True ownership means more than just completing tasks; it's about being proactive, investing ourselves fully in the outcomes, and fostering a culture of trust. Benefits & Reasons To Join Swapcard International team with 40+ nationalities (more on the way!) 🌍 Remote-first policy with headquarters in Paris 🗼 Thriving startup with career growth opportunities 🪴 Open-minded culture that appreciates differences 👽 Feedback-driven, supportive & curious team with a DIY mindset 🤔 🛠 Generous Paid Time Off to ensure you have time for what matters most ❤️🏡 Remote perks designed to optimize your working experience 🎁 In-person social gatherings to celebrate our achievements 🏝️ 100% of your health insurance contribution paid by Swapcard 🏥 Work-from-home budget (one-off contribution for equipment in addition to your initial equipment setup) 🖇️ Co-working space budget to support remote work in professional environments 💼 Learning budget to help you develop new and existing skills 🤓 Mental health care initiatives to support your well-being 🧘 Equal Opportunity Swapcard is committed to upholding equal employment practices and making merit-based employment decisions. title: Quality and Information Security Manager (M/F)
description: Company Description Accelerate your career within a fast-growing global engineering group. At SEGULA Technologies, you will work on exciting projects and help shape the future within a company where innovation is inseparable from engineering. New mobility, decarbonization, the factory of the future... these are the daily drivers for our 15,000 ingenious employee why not yours? Whether it's right next door or on the other side of the planet, you will find the opportunity at SEGULA Technologies that gives new meaning to your career! Job Description Core Mission To lead the design, implementation, and continuous improvement of a unified management system. This function ensures the organization meets global standards for quality, data security, environmental responsibility, and workplace safety, transforming compliance from a requirement into a competitive advantage. Quality Management System (ISO 9001) Process Excellence: Establish a process-based approach across all departments to ensure consistent output that meets client specifications. Risk-Based Thinking: Integrate risk identification into every project phase to prevent non-conformities before they occur. Quality Gate Architecture: Define specific technical validation points (Gates) throughout the product or project lifecycle. Performance Intelligence: Monitor the Cost of Poor Quality (COPQ) and use data-driven KPIs to trigger corrective action plans. Supplier Quality: Audit and rank vendors to ensure the entire supply chain aligns with the organization's quality benchmarks. Information Security & Automotive Data Trust (ISO 27001 & TISAX) Information Security Management (ISMS): Build a framework that protects the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data. Automotive Compliance (TISAX): Specifically address the high-security requirements of the automotive industry, focusing on prototype protection and third-party data handling. Vulnerability Governance: Lead regular risk assessments to identify technical and human threats to information assets. Incident Resilience: Develop a "Rapid Response" protocol for security breaches, ensuring minimal downtime and transparent reporting to stakeholders. Environmental, Health & Safety (ISO 14001 & ISO 45001) Environmental Stewardship (14001): Identify the organization's environmental aspects and impacts. Implement waste reduction, energy efficiency, and life-cycle thinking into operations. Workplace Safety (45001): Establish a proactive culture of health and safety. Conduct regular hazard identifications and "near-miss" reporting to eliminate workplace accidents. Legal Compliance: Maintain a rigorous regulatory watch to ensure the organization stays ahead of local and international environmental and labor laws. Emergency Preparedness: Design and test response plans for environmental accidents (spills) and medical or safety emergencies. Integrated Audit & Continuous Improvement Unified Audit Program: Plan and execute internal audits that cover all five standards (9001, 27001, TISAX, 14001, 45001) simultaneously to reduce operational disruption. Root Cause Mastery: Use structured methodologies (8D, 5 Whys, Ishikawa) to solve systemic failures across any of the management pillars. The PDCA Cycle: Drive the "Plan-Do-Check-Act" philosophy at every level, ensuring that "Lessons Learned" from one department are shared across the entire organization. Qualifications Functional Profile: IMS & Security Lead Professional Experience & Education Experience: 3 to 5 years of proven experience in Quality, Security, or Integrated Management Systems (IMS). Education: * Industrial Engineer or Quality Engineer degree. Master’s Degree in Quality, HSE, or Information Security Management. Languages: English and French are mandatory (Fluent/C1 level) for managing group standards and international audits. Standards Knowledge (The 5 Pillars) The core of this role is the mastery of the following international frameworks: Quality: ISO 9001 (Process approach and customer satisfaction). Information Security: ISO 27001 and TISAX (VDA ISA requirements for the automotive supply chain). Environment: ISO 14001 (Environmental impact and regulatory monitoring). Health & Safety: ISO 45001 (Risk prevention and workplace wellbeing). Technical Skills & Risk Assessment Risk Management (EBIOS RM): Expertise in the EBIOS Risk Manager methodology to identify security objectives, assess threats (mapping of digital risks), and define targeted safety measures. Integrated Auditing: Ability to conduct "Cross-Standard" internal audits to verify compliance across Quality, Security, and HSE simultaneously. Information Security Frameworks: Knowledge of physical security (access control), network security, and prototype protection protocols. Continuous Improvement: Proficiency in PDCA (Plan-Do-Check-Act) and structured problem-solving (8D, 5 Whys). Certifications ISO 9001 / 27001 / 14001 / 45001: Possession of a Lead Auditor or Lead Implementer certification is a significant advantage. TISAX: Evidence of specialized training in automotive security assessments. Core Soft Skills Change Management: Ability to drive the adoption of new standards and transform company culture toward a more secure and quality-focused mindset. Priority Management (Gestion des Priorités): Ability to balance urgent security alerts or safety incidents with long-term quality certification projects. Analytical Rigor: A systematic approach to documenting non-conformities and tracking corrective actions. Communication & Diplomacy: The ability to explain technical constraints to operational teams and present strategic risks to executive management. Adaptability: Comfortable working in a fast-paced industrial environment where standards and regulations evolve quickly. Additional Information Location: Tunis, Tunisia Type of contract: CDI All your information will remain confidential, in accordance with the EEO guidelines. #TN

title: Responsable Sécurité des Systèmes d’Information (RSSI)
description: Le groupe Outsourcia est un opérateur spécialisé dans les métiers de l'outsourcing: gestion de la relation client à distance, multiservice, multilingue et multicanal (centres d'appels, gestion des e-mails, solutions de chats, télémarketing et relation client sur les réseaux sociaux), externalisation de métiers de Back Office (BPO), prestations digitales (développement d'applications spécifiques, web ou mobiles, communication digitale et Community Management) et études. title: Data Consultant
description: Devoteam has been an international leader in consulting in digital strategy, technology platforms, cybersecurity and digital business transformation for 27 years. With more than 11,000 employees around the world, we imagine and realize their ambitions thanks to the infinite possibilities given by digital platforms, shaping a new culture and way of working. We are present in 25 countries, putting "Technology at the service of Man" in order to build a more sustainable world. Devoteam Tunisie, a subsidiary of Devoteam Group, has been generating value for over 13 years. Working on both the African and international scales, our team consists of over 100 multidisciplinary consultants (consultants in digital transformation, developers, project managers, data and cyber security experts) working on enriching and innovative projects. Job Description We are seeking our next talents to work on data-related projects (at Strategy, Business, and Operations levels). The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of data analysis, management, and visualization, coupled with strong problem-solving and communication skills. The Data Consultant will collaborate with clients and internal teams to assess data needs, develop strategies for effective data utilization, and implement solutions that drive business insights. Responsibilities: Analyze complex datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights. Interpret data to provide actionable recommendations for business improvement. Work closely with clients to understand their business goals and data requirements. Collaborate on the development of data strategies aligned with client objectives. Design and implement data management processes to ensure data accuracy, completeness, and security. Develop and maintain data documentation and metadata. Create visually appealing and insightful reports and dashboards. Communicate data findings effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Identify and resolve data-related issues and challenges. Propose innovative solutions to improve data processes and systems. Stay updated on industry trends and advancements in data technologies. Provide guidance on the selection and implementation of data tools and technologies. Qualifications Bachelor's or Master's degree in a relevant field (e.g., Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics). Proven 2 years experience as a Data Consultant or in a similar role. Technical: Proficiency in data analysis tools and programming languages such as Python, R, SQL, and/or other relevant tools. Strong understanding of data management, including data cleaning, preprocessing, and transformation. Experience with data visualization tools like Tableau, Power BI, or other similar tools. Problem Solving: Strong analytical and critical thinking skills to analyze complex business problems and provide data-driven solutions. Ability to identify patterns, trends, and insights from data to support strategic decision-making. Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills to effectively convey insights and recommendations to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Ability to communicate complex technical concepts in a clear and understandable manner. Good communication skills in english and arabic. Additional Information This role necessitate mobility flexibility, allowing individuals to work interchangeably between Tunis and Riyadh (Saudi Arabia).

Promouvoir la sensibilisation à la conformité et les comportements éthiques sur l'ensemble du site. Être le point de contact local pour les questions des employés relatives à la conformité. Contribuer à l'identification et à l'évaluation des risques de non-conformité spécifiques au site. Contribuer à la gestion des incidents, préoccupations et questions de conformité, en coordination avec le service Conformité global/régional. Apporter un soutien à la documentation, aux rapports et aux actions de suivi relatifs aux questions de conformité. Apporter un soutien aux audits, inspections et évaluations internes et externes liés à la conformité sur le site. Assurer la coordination avec le service Conformité global/régional Package attractif , Solide compréhension des principes de conformité en milieu industriel ou réglementé. Excellentes aptitudes à la communication et capacité à expliquer les sujets de conformité de manière claire et pratique. Intégrité, discrétion et discernement. Maîtrise de l'anglais , Michael Page recrute pour les entreprises des cadres confirmés, en CDD et CDI, grâce à l'expertise de 150 consultants répartis au sein de 17 divisions.

title: Document Analyst
description: Job Title: Document Analyst Location: Remote (Worldwide) Job Summary: The Document Analyst is responsible for reviewing, verifying, and analyzing documents to ensure accuracy, authenticity, completeness, and compliance with organizational policies and regulatory standards. This role supports operational integrity by identifying discrepancies, validating information, and assessing documentation for potential risk, fraud, or procedural gaps. Responsibilities Analyze and validate data extracted from a wide variety of documents, including government-issued IDs, proof of address, financial statements, legal contracts, and medical forms. Ensure extracted fields (name, date, document number, etc.) are accurate and complete. Identify sophisticated document fraud, including photoshopped IDs, counterfeit documents, and deepfake verification attempts. Use forensic analysis techniques to spot anomalies in security features (holograms, watermarks, microprint). Handle complex edge cases where documents are damaged, non-standard, or from unfamiliar jurisdictions. Use research skills and domain knowledge to make accurate determinations on document validity and data interpretation. Collaborate with Policy and Product teams to create, test, and refine annotation guidelines account for global document diversity and emerging fraud vectors. Provide structured feedback to machine learning engineers and data scientists on model performance. Identify systematic errors in OCR or data extraction and contribute to “golden datasets” used for model training and evaluation. Perform quality checks on document annotations and verifications completed by junior analysts or automated systems. Maintain high inter-annotator agreement (IAA) and ensure compliance with regulatory requirements. Ensure document processing workflows comply with global regulations, including KYC (Know Your Customer), AML (Anti-Money Laundering), GDPR, and eIDAS (Electronic Identification and Trust Services). Identify opportunities to streamline document review workflows, improve tooling, and reduce manual touchpoints without compromising accuracy or security. Requirements Minimum of 3 years of experience in Document Analysis, Identity verification, KYC/AML Operations, or Data Extraction within fintech, legal tech, trust & safety, or AI training sectors. Deep knowledge of global identity documents (passports, driver’s licenses, national IDs), financial documents (bank statements, pay stubs), and/or legal documents (contracts, incorporation papers). Proven ability to identify sophisticated document fraud, including manipulation techniques, forgery indicators, and biometric discrepancies. Experience with document verification tools, OCR software, or annotation platforms. Familiarity with data formats such as JSON, XML, or CSV for structured data output. Strong understanding of KYC/AML frameworks, data privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA) and industry standards for identity verification.

title: SRE w/Control-M - Relocation to Portugal
description: Your connection with Aubay starts in the following lines: Aubay Portugal is a French multinational company that has been operating in the country since 2007, with offices in Lisbon and Porto. We are a leading technology consultancy with a presence in seven European Union countries, specializing in the management, implementation, development and maintenance of information systems. With more than 150 active partners, we operate in strategic sectors such as banking, insurance, automotive, aviation, telecommunications, services and energy, driving innovation and digital transformation. Excellence, proximity and innovation are the pillars that distinguish us and enable us to support companies in building technological solutions that make a difference. What are we looking for? 3+ years of professional experience as Site Reliability Engineer; 1+ year of professional experience with Control-M; 1+ year of professional experience with Windows e Linux; 1+ year of professional experience with Cloud (AWS/Azure); 1+ year of professional experience with Kubernetes; Fluency in English and French (mandatory); Interest in working from Portugal (mandatory). What do we have to offer you? Comprehensive health insurance, including dental coverage; Feedback&Coffee – our integrated performance management system, with regular feedback sessions and a structured Career Progression Model; Aubay Training Academy, designed for your development: technical and behavioural training (in-class and e-learning), Meet&Techs, Webinars, Mentoring and Technology Centers; Events and team building activities that strengthen team spirit and promote closeness; Digital platform for mental health and emotional well-being, with free psychology consultations, so you can take care of yourself at any time; Health and well-being programme, with workshops and initiatives for our employees and other activities open to the community; We grow with you and your family - we offer a Mini-Aubilous Kit when your children are born and annual support for school supplies, from pre-school to higher education; Language platform, with conversation classes in 12 languages; Working model: hybrid/remote based in Portugal/on-site. What makes us different? Our organisational culture is based on solid values: commitment, excellence, team spirit, well-being, happiness and empathy; We value and recognise those who are part of our history, with referral awards and a special birthday gift; We believe in authenticity: we don't have a dress code, because what matters is talent and attitude; We promote closeness and an informal environment; Here, you will grow in good company, because we believe that professional development also means sharing experiences and building relationships. Your next adventure starts here! If you can see yourself growing with us, take the first step and send your CV! The future is waiting for you — and so are we! Personal Data collected by AUBAY PORTUGAL as Data Controller, regarding the identification, contact and professional capabilities of the candidates, in the present procedure, will be processed for the application analysis, having in consideration that the processing is conducted in order to to take the steps, at the request of the data subject, prior to entering into a contract; Personal data sent through the present application will be kept by Aubay during the application procedure and will remain stored whilst updated (for two years period). The collected data might be transferred or include other processing activities, as long as the data subject is informed, in the occurrence of a standard application and selection procedure, for the compliance of legal obligations for the time required by law. The Data Subject also has the right to file a complaint with the National Data Protection Authority – www.cnpd.pt. title: Data Governance Consultant
description: Devoteam is a leading consulting firm focused on digital strategy, tech platforms and cybersecurity. By combining creativity, tech and data insights, we empower our customers to transform their business and unlock the future. With 30 years’ experience and more than 12,000 employees across Europe, the Middle East and Africa, Devoteam promotes responsible tech for people and works to create better change. Our focus is to help our clients' win the digital battle, adapted to our clients’ business challenges, with a unique consideration to its impact on their systems and structures. Job Description Design and implement the data governance framework, operating model, and roles and responsibilities. Develop and implement data governance process workflows. Excellent communication, analytical, and stakeholder management skills. Ability to work collaboratively in a cross-functional environment. title: Security Operations Engineer
description: We are growing! We are looking for a Security Operations Engineer who can work with in a hybrid or a remote set-up. For this role, we are specifically looking for candidates based in Tunisia or Lebanon. Who we are: Founded in 2006, we’re proud to be a global business. From Shanghai to Paris, we have 12 offices and operate across four continents in 70 countries. We are home to over 250 professionals from around the world, working together to serve more than 230 luxury clients. At CXG, we love to evolve, elevate, and transform experiences while bringing brand promises to life. We offer strategic solutions that impact performance and elevate the customer experience of some of the world’s most iconic premium and luxury brands. What you will be doing: The Security Operations Engineer is responsible for operating, maintaining, and improving the organization’s security controls across cloud and enterprise environments. This role focuses on hands-on execution of security operations, ensuring that vulnerabilities are identified, security tools are effectively configured, and incidents are properly handled. The objective is to strengthen day-to-day security posture through structured processes, automation, and collaboration with engineering and IT teams. Your duties will also involve : Security Monitoring & Operations Monitor security alerts and investigate potential threats Operate and maintain security tools and platforms Support incident detection, analysis, and response activities Ensure proper logging, alerting, and escalation mechanisms are in place Vulnerability Management & VAPT Run regular vulnerability scans using appropriate tools Support coordination of periodic penetration testing (e.g., quarterly or risk-based) Track vulnerabilities and ensure remediation follow-up Assist in validating fixes and reducing exposure over time Application Security Run and maintain automated security testing tools (SAST, DAST, dependency scanning) Integrate security checks into development workflows Support developers in identifying and fixing vulnerabilities Contribute to improving secure development practices Automation-first approach. policies Assist in ensuring secure collaboration and data sharing Third-Party & Vendor Security Governance Support security assessments of vendors and SaaS tools Assist in completing security risks related to AI-based solutions Ensure basic security requirements are considered during tool adoption Incident Preparedness & Response Participate in security incident handling and investigations teams Support internal guidance on secure practices Continuously improve processes, tools, and controls Stay updated on emerging threats and security practices What you will bring along: Minimum 3 title: Security Engineer Annotator
description: Job Summary: We are looking for a Security Engineer Annotator to evaluate cybersecurity datasets used in AI model training. The ideal candidate will analyze, security related datasets, code samples, architectural designs. The Security Engineer role involves identifying vulnerabilities, labeling security scenarios, and validating AI-generated security recommendations. Job Responsibilities Annotate cybersecurity datasets and threat scenarios. Evaluate secure coding practices and vulnerability assessments. Label datasets involving penetration testing and risk analysis. Review authentication, encryption, and access control implementations. Assess AI responses related to security threats and mitigation. Strong understanding of secure software development. Knowledge of network security, cryptography, and vulnerability management. Experience with penetration testing tools. title: Information Management Specialist
description: Danish Refugee Council, one of the world’s leading humanitarian NGOs, is currently looking for a highly qualified Information Management Specialist (IM Specialist) to support our DRC P2P program under a consortium that has it’s Coordination unit in Tunis. About The Job The Information Management Specialist (IM Specialist) will lead data management for the P2P consortium, implemented by DRC, Danish Red Cross, and IDC across 10 countries. He/She will oversee beneficiary databases, ensure data accuracy, support MEAL teams with reporting, and develop tools for data collection and analysis. The role includes mentoring and training field and partner teams on data quality and protection standards. Additionally, the IM Specialist will produce dashboards, reports, and trend analyses to support internal and external information sharing. Your main duties and responsibilities will be: Lead the design, roll-out and implementation of IM/knowledge management systems for different activities implemented by the consortium. In coordination with technical coordinators, support with the design/management of trackers and databases for all activities, including those implemented through partners. Act as the main technical focal point in the administration and roll-out of the beneficiary information management (IM) system and support the development of related guidance/SOPs and data protection measures. Provide day-to-day technical support to field and partner teams, including delivering trainings and refresher trainings on the use of IM systems. Collaborate with area office teams, MEAL/IM teams, and program teams in country offices to ensure appropriate data management systems are in place and operationalised. Assist with developing data collection tools for quantitative and qualitative research related to consortium activities in cooperation with the technical sectors and MEAL Coordinator. Generate reports, trend analyses, and information updates for various audiences using data from DRC sectors/partners IM systems; assist in consolidating and analysing trends and key findings of primary and secondary data. Support the MEAL and program teams with compiling beneficiary lists for service provision, verification, and monitoring. Coordinate with country teams to ensure all activity trackers/databases (including beneficiary and target trackers) are up to date; format and clean data, highlight inconsistencies or errors. Develop and maintain a monthly cumulative dashboard to track beneficiaries reached under different interventions (including through partners) and keep indicator trackers up to date for timely and accurate donor reporting.Keeping indicator trackers up to date for all the locations and share timely and accurate figures with the CMU, country MEAL and grants for donor reporting. About you : To be successful in this role we expect you to have at least 3 years of relevant working experience in Information Management within the humanitarian field Moreover, we also expect the following: Demonstrated experience with developing and managing beneficiary information management systems and relational databases in general Previous experience with ActivityInfo in particular is an advantage High level of proficiency with developing automated PowerBI dashboards High level of proficiency in Word, PowerPoint, SharePoint, Excel Experience with analyzing qualitative and quantitative data High level of proficiency with KoBo toolbox Experience with advanced statistical analysis software (SPSS, R, Stata,…) is an advantage Proficiency with R Script is an advantage Experience with protection and economic recovery programming is an advantage Capacity to produce high-quality graphic oriented reports and analysis Experience in working in complex and volatile contexts Experience with organising trainings for both technical IM staff and non-technical experts Fluency in English, French and Arabic (both written and verbal) In this position, you are expected to demonstrate DRC’ five core competencies: Striving for excellence: You focus on reaching results while ensuring an efficient process. Collaborating: You involve relevant parties and encourage feedback. Taking the lead: You take ownership and initiative while aiming for innovation. Communicating: You listen and speak effectively and honestly. Demonstrating integrity: You uphold and promote the highest standards of ethical and professional conduct in relation to our values and Code of Conduct, including safeguarding against sexual exploitation, abuse and harassment. We offer Contract length: Open-ended contract with 6 months probation period Band: NMG-G2 Designation of Duty Station: Tunis Start date: May 2026. Salary and conditions will be in accordance with Danish Refugee Council’s Terms of Employment for Expatriates; please refer to drc.ngo under Vacancies . For questions regarding the vacancy please contact [Samme Tom Dick at samme.dick@drc.ngo]. Application process All applicants must upload a cover letter and an updated CV (no longer than four pages) in English. Applications sent by email will not be considered. Closing date for applications: 28 April 2026. For further information about the Danish Refugee Council, please consult our website drc.ngo .

title: Ingénieur en sécurité des applications senior (F/H)
description: Construisez la sécurité au cœur d’une transformation SaaS européenne Contexte & Missions Dans le cadre de sa croissance internationale, Orisha , éditeur européen de logiciels SaaS B2B, renforce ses capacités technologiques et poursuit la structuration de son organisation engineering. Pour accompagner cette dynamique, le groupe lance la création d’un hub technologique à Tunis , destiné à soutenir plusieurs Business Units à l’échelle européenne. Dans ce contexte, nous recherchons un Application Security Engineer pour jouer un rôle clé dans l’intégration de la sécurité au cœur des pratiques de développement du groupe. Ce poste n’est pas un rôle d’audit ou de conformité. Il s’agit d’un rôle hands-on, orienté build , visant à intégrer la sécurité dès les premières phases du cycle de développement ( approche “Shift Left” ) et à accompagner les équipes techniques dans la réduction des vulnérabilités à la source. Votre futur quotidien chez nous En tant qu’Application Security Engineer, vous interviendrez comme un partenaire clé des équipes de développement , avec un rôle transverse à l’échelle du groupe. Vos principales responsabilités : Intégrer la sécurité dans les pipelines CI/CD (approche DevSecOps) Déployer et maintenir des outils d’analyse de sécurité ( SAST, DAST, SCA ) Analyser et prioriser les vulnérabilités, en réduisant les faux positifs Accompagner les équipes de développement dans la correction des failles au niveau du code Exploiter les résultats des tests d’intrusion (pentests) Participer aux phases de conception produit ( threat modeling ) Définir et promouvoir des standards et bonnes pratiques de sécurité Animer une communauté de Security Champions Vous évoluerez dans un environnement multi-produits, international et orienté delivery , en collaboration étroite avec les équipes engineering, produit et sécurité du groupe. Et si c’était vous ? Profil recherché ✔ Vous avez un background solide en développement logiciel, DevSecOps ou sécurité applicative ✔ Vous êtes à l’aise dans des environnements CI/CD, cloud et automatisation ✔ Vous avez déjà travaillé avec des outils de sécurité applicative ( SAST, DAST, SCA ) ✔ Vous comprenez les enjeux de sécurisation du SDLC , au-delà de l’audit ✔ Vous aimez travailler au contact des développeurs et influencer les pratiques techniques ✔ Vous êtes pragmatique, orienté impact et à l’aise dans des environnements en structuration Langues ✔ Français courant ✔ Anglais courant Serait un plus ✔ Expérience avec des outils comme GitLab CI, Black Duck, Jira ✔ Connaissance des frameworks de sécurité type CIS Ce que vous gagnerez dans cette aventure Un rôle stratégique avec une forte visibilité à l’échelle du groupe L’opportunité de structurer les pratiques AppSec from scratch Une exposition à un environnement SaaS multi-produits international Un impact direct sur la qualité des produits et la posture sécurité globale Une culture basée sur la responsabilisation, la collaboration et l’excellence technique Pourquoi rejoindre Orisha ? Rejoindre Orisha, c’est : ✨ Participer à la construction d’un hub technologique stratégique en Tunisie ✨ Intégrer un groupe SaaS européen en forte croissance ✨ Contribuer à une transformation technologique ambitieuse ✨ Jouer un rôle clé dans la sécurisation de produits à fort impact Postulez dès maintenant Rejoignez-nous pour construire des plateformes SaaS sécurisées, scalables et modernes, au cœur de la transformation d’Orisha.
