title: Operation Technologies & Compliance Specialist
description: SIT S.p.a., à travers les deux divisions Chauffage et Smart Gas Metering, a créé des solutions intelligentes pour contrôler les conditions environnementales et mesurer la consommation pour un monde plus durable. Multinational leader sur les marchés de référence et cotée sur le segment MTA de la Bourse italienne, SIT veut être le principal partenaire durable des solutions d'énergie et de climatisation au service des entreprises clientes, en accordant une grande attention à l'expérimentation et impact environnemental. Le Groupe est présent avec des sites de production en Italie, au Mexique, en Hollande, en Roumanie et en Chine, ainsi qu'une structure commerciale qui couvre tous les marchés mondiaux de référence. L'entreprise recherche la ressource suivante : Operation Technologies & Compliance Specialist Missions: Ce poste consiste à gérer et maintenir l'infrastructure informatique de l'usine. Le technicien assurera le bon fonctionnement des systèmes informatiques de production (serveurs, MES, postes de travail) et l'intégration des applications logicielles et des automates programmables, contribuant ainsi à l'efficacité des processus de production, aux mises à niveau des lignes de production pour répondre aux nouvelles exigences des clients et à la sécurité du système informatique. Gérer et maintenir la stabilité des serveurs et des systèmes informatiques. Configurer et assurer le support des PC de production et de post-production. Contrôler et superviser le système MES (Manufacturing Execution System). Assurer le support et la gestion de l'intégration des applications PC et PLC pour la traçabilité et le contrôle des processus. Élaborer les spécifications pour la mise en œuvre de nouvelles fonctions de contrôle et de traçabilité. Mettre en œuvre une politique de sécurité informatique pour protéger le périmètre de production. Appliquer les réglementations et les bonnes pratiques en matière de sécurité informatique (NIS2, ISO 27001, IEC 62443). Résoudre les problèmes techniques et apporter un support aux utilisateurs internes. Collaborer avec l'équipe d'automatisation et de maintenance pour garantir la continuité des activités. Mettre en œuvre les procédures de sécurité informatique et de sauvegarde. Profil recherché: • Licence ou Master en informatique, génie informatique, automatisation industrielle ou cybersécurité. • Certifications professionnelles : o Auditeur principal ISO 27001 (sécurité de l’information). o Certification IEC 62443 (sécurité des systèmes d’automatisation industrielle). o Certifications en automates programmables et automatisation (par exemple, Siemens, Rockwell). • Formations spécifiques sur la norme NIS2 et la conformité réglementaire pour les infrastructures critiques. • Au moins 3 à 5 ans d’expérience dans un contexte industriel ou métallurgique. • Gestion d’infrastructures informatiques complexes (serveurs, réseaux, systèmes MES et ERP). • Projets d’intégration IT/OT (PC ↔ API, protocoles industriels tels que OPC UA et Modbus). • Mise en œuvre de solutions de cybersécurité industrielle (segmentation du réseau, pare-feu industriels, IDS/IPS). • Participation à des projets de numérisation d’usine (Industrie 4.0, IoT industriel).

title: Privileged Access Management Engineer
description: We are seeking a skilled Privileged Access Management (PAM) Engineer to design, implement, and maintain our PAM solution. PAM Engineer needs to have a deep understanding of privileged access security principles and experience working with industry-leading PAM tools. This role will involve integrating PAM systems into our IT infrastructure, automating processes, and ensuring the security and compliance of privileged accounts across the organization. Key Responsibilities: PAM Implementation & Maintenance: Design, deploy, and configure PAM solutions such as Wallix, CyberArk, BeyondTrust. Integrate PAM systems with existing IT infrastructure, including Active Directory, LDAP, and cloud environments. Ensure the scalability, availability, and performance of PAM systems. Privileged Account Management: Automate privileged account provisioning, rotation, and de-provisioning processes. Manage and monitor privileged accounts, credentials, and access permissions across all systems. Security & Compliance: Enforce privileged access policies and ensure compliance with regulatory standards. Perform regular audits of privileged account activities and provide detailed reports. Monitor PAM logs for anomalies, unauthorized access, or potential security incidents. Required Skills: Expertise in deploying and managing PAM tools. Strong understanding of authentication protocols (e.g., Kerberos, SAML, OAuth) and directory services (e.g., Active Directory, LDAP) and Public Key Infrastructure Hands-on experience with OS: -Windows Server: RDS,NPS,Radius -Linux: Basic commands Scripting and automation skills (e.g., PowerShell or Bash) Knowledge of cybersecurity principles, including least privilege, zero trust, and privileged access controls. Our momentum is reflected in our achievements and customer satisfaction: CNBC Disruptor 50, Forbes Cloud 100, Deloitte Fast 500, and repeated recognition on Y Combinator’s top companies list – all while maintaining a 4.83 average rating from 15,000 reviews across G2, Trustpilot, Captera, Apple and Google. Your experience at Deel will be a career accelerator. At the forefront of the global work revolution, you'll tackle complex challenges that impact millions of people's working lives. With our momentum—backed by a $17.3 billion valuation and $1 B in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) in just over five years—you'll drive meaningful impact while building expertise that makes you a sought-after leader in the transformation of global work. Team Lead About The Team At the core of Deel’s FinTech platform, responsible for building and operating client and contractor wallets across our regulated entities globally. This is a mission-critical domain where accuracy, compliance, and reliability are non-negotiable. Scope & Responsibilities Lead the fintech engineering team to design, build, and scale wallet infrastructure for global deel's users. Work with different teams to integrate wallets with regional banks through PSPs (via APIs), orchestrating pay-ins and ensuring seamless treasury operations. Depending on the project, you may collaborate with the Core Accounting team to connect wallets with the General Ledger (GL), ensuring balances are accurate and financial records meet audit standards. Work closely with Compliance, Treasury, and PSP Integration teams to ensure adherence to local regulations (MTL, PI, safeguarding requirements). Provide technical leadership in a global, fast-paced environment where uptime and precision are critical. Responsible for leading a high-performing engineering team, including running daily stand-ups, planning sessions, and backlog grooming. What We’re Looking For Location: Based in EMEA, with high availability for global collaboration. Background: Proven experience as a Team Lead or Manager in payments, banking, accounting, or treasury. FinTech Expertise: Hands-on wallets, flows of funds, compliance, and regulated money movement. Prior experience at companies like Wise, PayPal, or similar is a strong plus. Technical Skills: JavaScript/Node.js PostgreSQL with Sequelize ORM Strong understanding of distributed systems and API-driven banking/PSP integrations Leadership: Ability to run projects at scale, mentor engineers, and drive cross-functional alignment. Mindset: Ownership, detail-oriented, and mindful of the responsibility of managing other people’s money. Important note: This role will remain technically hands-on, with responsibilities divided between people management and hands-on tasks. Why This Role Matters This team is the foundation of Deel’s regulated money movement. The work ensures client funds are safe, compliant, and seamlessly accessible worldwide Total Rewards Our workforce deserves fair and competitive pay that meets them where they are. With scalable benefits, rewards, and perks, our total rewards programs reflect our commitment to inclusivity and access for all. Some things you’ll enjoy Stock grant opportunities dependent on your role, employment status and location Additional perks and benefits based on your employment status and country The flexibility of remote work, including optional WeWork access At Deel, we’re an equal-opportunity employer that values diversity and positively encourage applications from suitably qualified and eligible candidates regardless of race, religion, sex, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status, pregnancy or maternity or other applicable legally protected characteristics. Deel will provide accommodations on request throughout the recruitment, selection and assessment process for applicants with disabilities. title: Ingénieur Performance & Observabilité (Dynatrace)
description: Chez Devoteam, nous sommes des « Digital Transformakers ». Le respect, la franchise et la passion animent chaque jour notre tribu. Ensemble, nous aidons nos clients à remporter la bataille du Digital : du conseil à la mise en œuvre de technologies innovantes, jusqu’à l’adoption des usages. Cloud, Cybersecurity, Data, DevOps, Fullstack Dev, Low Code, RPA n’ont plus aucun secret pour notre tribu ! Nos 12 000+ collaborateurs sont certifiés, formés et accompagnés au quotidien pour relever de nouveaux challenges innovants. Leader du Cloud, de la Cybersécurité et de la Data en EMEA, le Groupe Devoteam a réalisé en 2023 un chiffre d’affaires de 1,3 milliard d’euros et se donne pour ambition de le doubler dans les 5 années à venir. Devoteam Tunisie, filiale de Devoteam Group, est génératrice de valeur depuis plus de 15 années. Travaillant à la fois à l'échelle africaine et internationale, notre équipe compte plus de 170 consultant(e)s pluridisciplinaires doté(e)s de compétences variées (consultants en transformation digitale, développeurs, chefs de projets, experts en data et cybersécurité) travaillant sur des projets enrichissants et innovants. Etes-vous prêt(e) à nous rejoindre et relever ensemble ce défi ? Description Du Poste Rattaché(e) à l’équipe Observabilité / SRE , votre mission est de garantir une expérience utilisateur optimale et proactive. Vous ne vous contentez pas de tester si le code fonctionne : vous concevez des dispositifs de surveillance intelligents qui simulent les parcours critiques de nos utilisateurs 24h/24 et 7j/7, afin de détecter les régressions et les baisses de performance avant même qu'elles n'impactent nos clients. Automatisation & Monitoring Synthétique (Cœur du métier) Conception : Identifier les parcours utilisateurs critiques (Golden Paths) et les traduire en scénarios de tests fonctionnels. Implémentation : Créer et configurer des moniteurs synthétiques (Browser & HTTP) au sein de Dynatrace. Scripting Avancé : Développer des scripts robustes en JavaScript pour gérer les cas complexes : authentifications multi-facteurs (MFA), manipulations de cookies, gestion du DOM et validation de données dynamiques. Intégration dans la Culture DevOps Shift-Left Testing : Intégrer le déclenchement des tests synthétiques directement dans nos pipelines CI/CD (GitLab CI, Jenkins ou Azure DevOps) pour valider les releases dès les environnements de pré-production. Collaboration SRE : Travailler main dans la main avec les équipes Ops/SRE pour affiner les alertes et réduire le "bruit" (fausses alertes). Maintenance et Amélioration Continue Assurer la maintenance évolutive des scripts face aux changements d'interface (UI) des applications. Optimiser les temps d'exécution des tests et la pertinence des métriques remontées. Qualifications Hard-skills : Maîtrise de Dynatrace : Expérience solide sur le module Synthetic Monitoring (via interface et API). Développement / Scripting : Excellente maîtrise du JavaScript (indispensable pour les scénarios avancés). Culture Web & API : Bonne compréhension de l'architecture web (HTML/CSS, sélecteurs DOM, requêtes HTTP/REST). Automatisation & CI/CD : Connaissance des outils de versioning (Git) et de l'intégration continue. Méthodologie QA : Maîtrise des principes de tests (fonctionnels, non-fonctionnels, non-régression). Atouts supplémentaires : Expérience sur des outils de tests d'API (Postman) ou d'autres frameworks d'automation (Tosca, Playwright, Selenium). Soft-skills : Pédagogie & Communication : Capacité à expliquer des enjeux techniques complexes (performance, disponibilité) à des interlocuteurs métiers ou produits. Proactivité : Force de proposition pour étendre la couverture de test et anticiper les pannes. Expérience : Minimum 2 à 3 ans sur un poste similaire en Automation de tests ou Monitoring applicatif (APM). Français & Anglais : Capacité à évoluer dans un environnement technique international. Informations supplémentaires Diplômé(e) d’un Bac+3 / Bac+3 en école d'ingénieur ou équivalent universitaire avec une spécialisation en informatique, ou justifiant d'une expérience significative équivalente Expérience de plus de 3 ans dans un poste similaire

Proficiency in SQL, Excel, and reporting toolsis a plus. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Excellent communication and customer service skills. Ability to work in a fast-paced environmentand handle multiple priorities. Preferred Qualifications Experience in ERP implementation projects. Knowledge of ERP security, workflows, and integrations. Certification in ERP systems or ITIL service management. Familiarity with data privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA, etc.). Join Questel, a growing international group, and live a great human adventure!

title: SRE / SecOps Engineer

The opportunity to work in exciting contexts and to help generate a positive impact around the world. Career Growth opportunity Healthcare and wellness Benefits to you and your family An evolving and pleasant environment that challenges you to develop new skills Appropriate conditions to help you focus on what you love doing A winning team that has relied on Respect, Sharing, and Passion for what it does Equal Employment Opportunities no matter your gender, religious, culture, etc Your Next Steps If you have the ambition to stimulate your career with new challenges while capitalizing on your potential and your perpetual thirst for learning, this job is for you. title: Tech Lead, Engineering
description: Who We Are Is What We Do. Deel is the all-in-one payroll and HR platform for global teams. Our vision is to unlock global opportunity for every person, team, and business. Built for the way the world works today, Deel combines HRIS, payroll, compliance, benefits, performance, and equipment management into one seamless platform. With AI-powered tools and a fully owned payroll infrastructure, Deel supports every worker type in 150+ countries—helping businesses scale smarter, faster, and more compliantly. Among the largest globally distributed companies in the world, our team of 7,000 spans more than 100 countries, speaks 74 languages, and brings a connected and dynamic culture that drives continuous learning and innovation for our customers. Why should you be part of our success story? As the fastest-growing Software as a Service (SaaS) company in history, Deel is transforming how global talent connects with world-class companies – breaking down borders that have traditionally limited both hiring and career opportunities. We're not just building software; we're creating the infrastructure for the future of work, enabling a more diverse and inclusive global economy. In 2024 alone, we paid $11.2 billion to workers in nearly 100 currencies and provided healthcare and benefits to workers in 109 countries—ensuring people get paid and protected, no matter where they are. Our momentum is reflected in our achievements and customer satisfaction: CNBC Disruptor 50, Forbes Cloud 100, Deloitte Fast 500, and repeated recognition on Y Combinator’s top companies list – all while maintaining a 4.83 average rating from 15,000 reviews across G2, Trustpilot, Captera, Apple and Google. Your experience at Deel will be a career accelerator. At the forefront of the global work revolution, you'll tackle complex challenges that impact millions of people's working lives. With our momentum—backed by a $17.3 billion valuation and $1 B in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) in just over five years—you'll drive meaningful impact while building expertise that makes you a sought-after leader in the transformation of global work. We’re looking for a Tech Lead with deep fulltack and database expertise , someone who understands how systems behave under load, how data moves, and how to make the right trade-offs. You won’t be only measured by how well you write Node.js, you’ll be measured by: The problems you choose to solve The decisions you make around data and architecture Your ability to lead through influence and drive outcomes What You’ll Do Identify and prioritize high-impact technical and product problems Design and guide scalable backend and data systems Own architectural decisions across data, services, and system boundaries Ensure systems are efficient, reliable, and scalable under real-world conditions Lead execution through others (not just individual contribution) Partner with Product to ensure technical work drives business outcomes What Matters Most Deep Database Expertise (Critical) Strong knowledge of relational databases (SQL) Experience with: Query optimization Indexing strategies Transactions & isolation levels Concurrency control Ability to reason about performance and trade-offs at the data layer This is the most important technical requirement and a known gap in most candidates Distributed Systems & System Design Strong understanding of: Queues, message brokers, async processing Microservices and service boundaries Load balancing, CDNs, and request lifecycle Ability to design end-to-end systems, not just isolated services Backend Foundations Strong fundamentals in: APIs, services, and system architecture OOP and clean design principles Node.js / TypeScript knowledge You should be able to work in it, but it is not the differentiator Product & Business Thinking Understands why something should be built, not just how Makes pragmatic decisions (80/20 mindset) aligned with business impact Avoids over-engineering Technical Leadership Through Influence Leads through clarity, reasoning, and trust, not authority Can drive alignment across engineers and stakeholders Delegates effectively and scales impact through others Working knowledge of frontend (React) : Understands performance implications and integration points Not expected to focus on UI or design Strong communication: Explains technical trade-offs clearly to both engineers and non-technical stakeholders What makes you stand out: The Tech Lead we need: Finds the right problems Prioritizes them based on business impact Designs solutions and drives execution through the team This Role Might Not Be a Fit If… You have Weak understanding of SQL, transactions, or concurrency Talks about tools/frameworks but lacks systems thinking Focuses on technical complexity over impact Relies on authority instead of influence Over-engineers instead of making pragmatic trade-offs Cannot delegate or scale through others Total Rewards Our workforce deserves fair and competitive pay that meets them where they are. With scalable benefits, rewards, and perks, our total rewards programs reflect our commitment to inclusivity and access for all. Some things you’ll enjoy Stock grant opportunities dependent on your role, employment status and location Additional perks and benefits based on your employment status and country The flexibility of remote work, including optional WeWork access At Deel, we’re an equal-opportunity employer that values diversity and positively encourage applications from suitably qualified and eligible candidates regardless of race, religion, sex, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status, pregnancy or maternity or other applicable legally protected characteristics. Unless otherwise agreed, we will communicate with job applicants using Deel-specific emails, which include @ deel.com and other acquired company emails like @ payspace.com and @ paygroup.com . You can view the most up-to-date job listings at Deel by visiting our careers page . Deel is an equal-opportunity employer and is committed to cultivating a diverse and inclusive workplace that reflects different abilities, backgrounds, beliefs, experiences, identities and perspectives. Deel will provide accommodations on request throughout the recruitment, selection and assessment process for applicants with disabilities. If you require accommodations, please inform our Talent Acquisition Team via this link and a team member will be in touch to ensure your equal participation. If you have difficulty accessing the form, please email at recruiting@deel.com. As part of our hiring process, we primarily rely on interviews and role-related assessments. In limited cases, we may also consider informal background information relevant to the role, in line with our privacy and fairness obligations. This application process does utilise Automated Employment Decision Tools (AEDT) and AI systems to assist in evaluating candidates based on experience level, technical skills and qualifications. As a fully remote company, we also utilise AI-powered deepfake and fraud detection technologies to verify the authenticity of candidate identities and interactions during assessments and interviews. This processing is conducted in compliance with applicable Data Protection, AI Governance and Labour Laws. We ensure human oversight is maintained in all final hiring decisions. title: SysOps Engineer

At ITRS, we make society's critical technology work by delivering automated, holistic IT observability solutions trusted by 90% of Tier 1 capital markets firms and thousands of organizations globally. With headquarters in London and teams across the US, Europe, and Asia, ITRS combines the agility of a high-impact tech business with the stability of a private equity-backed global partner. Deployment : Deploy and configure measurement networks across various environments, including Cloud (AWS/Azure) and Physical infrastructure (Systems & Networks). Administration : Administer and maintain system and network architectures to ensure high availability. Operations & Security Monitoring : Supervise systems and applications to proactively identify performance bottlenecks or failures. Troubleshooting : Diagnose and resolve system outages or malfunctions as they occur. Security : Implement and manage IT security protocols related to SysOps activities to protect data integrity. Knowledge Management : Document operational procedures and contribute to the internal knowledge base to improve team efficiency. Work Conditions Shift Work : Ability to work night shifts and rotating schedules to ensure 24/7 operational continuity. On-call : Availability for occasional on-call support during weekends or holidays if required. Requirements Education & Experience Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field. A first experience (internship or initial role) in systems administration or IT support. Technical Potential & Certifications The candidate will have the opportunity to pursue or should ideally possess interest in the following certifications : AWS : Cloud Practitioner / SysOps Administrator Associate. Azure : Fundamentals / Azure Administrator Associate. Linux : Red Hat Certified Systems Administrator (RHCSA). Key Competencies Basic understanding of Cloud computing (AWS/Azure). Familiarity with network protocols and system security. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Excellent documentation skills and a proactive approach to learning. title: Automation QA Engineer - Playwright
description: Job Description We are seeking a highly skilled Quality Assurance Engineer to join our team. The ideal candidate will have strong experience in automated testing, expertise in Playwright for end-to-end testing, and an ISTQB certification. You will work closely with developers, product managers, and DevOps engineers to ensure high-quality software delivery. Responsibilities Design, develop, and maintain automated test scripts using Playwright (or other frameworks). Conduct functional, regression, performance, and security testing across web applications. Work closely with developers to integrate testing into the CI/CD pipeline. Implement and maintain a test automation framework. Identify, document, and track bugs using JIRA, Azure DevOps, or similar tools. Ensure test coverage for APIs, UI, and databases. Perform manual testing when necessary to validate complex scenarios. Develop test plans, test cases, and test reports aligned with project requirements. Stay updated on new trends in software testing and automation. Requirements 3+ years of experience in software testing and automation. ISTQB Foundation Level certification (Advanced Level is a plus). Strong experience with Playwright, Selenium, or Cypress. Experience with API testing (Postman, RestAssured, or similar). Knowledge of programming languages such as JavaScript, TypeScript, or Python. Experience working in Agile/Scrum environments. Familiarity with CI/CD tools like Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI/CD, or Azure DevOps. Strong understanding of software development lifecycle (SDLC) and QA best practices. Experience with performance testing tools (JMeter, k6) is a plus. Excellent problem-solving and communication skills. Nice to Have Knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP). Familiarity with containerized environments (Docker, Kubernetes).

title: Senior Data Scientist
description: We are one of the leading technology groups in Italy. We operate as a Business & Technology Accelerator for companies, financial institutions, and public sector organizations, supporting the evolution of their IT and digital strategies. Through an ecosystem of companies that share values, business strategies, and a common vision, we enable our clients to unlock the full potential of technology, amplifying its positive impact on both the economy and society. What To Expect We are looking for a Data Scientist capable of bridging the gap between vague business questions and rigorous mathematical answers. You must be capable of digging into messy data to find the “truth” and defining what problems should (and can) be solved. You will own the scientific strategy, determining which problems are solvable and proving that our solutions actually work. As our Senior Data Scientist, you will act as the Strategic Lead of the Applied Science Track. You will report to the Head of Engineering/Principal Scientist and define the roadmap for our Machine Learning, NLP, and Optimization (Operations Research) initiatives. Crucially, this is a hands-on leadership role. You will be the "Architect" who defines the problem, but you will also actively get your hands dirty with data analysis to validate feasibility. You define the work for the Applied Scientist (Optimization), the Machine Learning Engineer (the Builder), and the NLP Specialist (the Domain Expert). Your goal is to ensure we are solving the right problems with the right data, avoiding the trap of "AI looking for a use case." What You Will Do You will collaborate with Product Managers to translate abstract business goals (e.g., "reduce downtime") into concrete mathematical problems (e.g., "predict failure probability," "minimize scheduling cost," or "categorize maintenance logs automatically"). You will dive into large, complex industrial datasets to uncover hidden patterns, trends, and anomalies before any modeling begins. For Optimization projects, you define the "Business Objective" (e.g., "balance cost vs. speed") that the Applied Scientist will encode into the mathematical solver. You ensure the math solves the actual business need. You will design and execute experiments (A/B testing, hypothesis testing) to validate that our models are performing better than random chance. You prevent the team from being fooled by noise. You will build the initial "Proof of Concept" models to test if a solution is possible. Once validated, you hand off the logic to the Engineers/Scientists for production hardening. You define the "Definition of Success" (accuracy, precision/recall, ROI) that the rest of the technical team must aim for. What We Expect From You 5+ years of experience in Data Science/Applied Statistics, comfortable working with messy, unstructured data. Expert in Python and SQL (Pandas, Scikit-Learn, Statsmodels). Strong statistical foundation (probability, hypothesis testing, causal inference) with sound judgment on when not to use deep learning. Industrial/MRO exposure, including time-series, sensor data, or logistics. Experience mentoring/leading a small technical team and collaborating across Operations Research, NLP (LLMs, embeddings, RAG), and Engineering, mindful of latency and cost constraints.

title: Golang Full Stack Developer
description: We are one of the leading technology groups in Italy. We operate as a Business & Technology Accelerator for companies, financial institutions, and public sector organizations, supporting the evolution of their IT and digital strategies. Through an ecosystem of companies that share values, business strategies, and a common vision, we enable our clients to unlock the full potential of technology, amplifying its positive impact on both the economy and society. What To Expect We are a leading cyber security consultancy and Continuous Controls Monitoring (CCM) provider dedicated to helping organisations protect their digital assets and infrastructure. Our innovative solutions, expert consultancy and bespoke CCM services are designed to meet the unique security needs of businesses across various industries. The role provides a fundamental part of the Quod Orbis team. As a Developer at our company, you will be involved in the full software development lifecycle, from concept and design to testing and deployment. You will work closely with our team of developers, product managers, and cyber security experts to create high-quality, scalable, and secure SaaS solutions. The Developer will have a strong background in software development, with a passion for technology and a keen interest in cyber security. What You Will Do Drive the technical implementation of the move from single-tenant to multi-tenant architecture. Work closely with the Head of Platform to plan, design, and execute the migration strategy. Develop tenant-aware backend services and APIs in Go. Support data migration, including schema changes, backfills, and transition from existing environments. Implement and test tenant isolation, access control, and configuration management. Update frontend components and routing to support tenant context where needed. Improve database performance, partitioning, and query efficiency for multi-tenant systems. Enhance observability, logging, and monitoring with tenant-level visibility. Collaborate with DevOps to achieve zero-downtime releases and safe rollouts. Mentor backend developers and promote best practices across the team (SAST/DAST). What We Expect From You Contact with others (face-to-face, by telephone or otherwise, including visits to other sites) Attention to detail and a high level of accuracy 6+ years of backend development experience. Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field. A Master’s degree or relevant certifications are a desirable but not mandatory. Strong Go development experience, ideally in distributed or high-scale systems. Solid frontend experience. Proven experience with multi-tenant system design or migration projects. Deep understanding of tenancy models; database per tenant, schema per tenant, or shared schema and the trade-offs of each. Skilled in data modelling, schema partitioning, and tenant-aware querying. Experience implementing tenant-based authentication, authorisation, and RBAC. Experience in migrating legacy applications or environments into shared, multi-tenant platforms. Comfortable working with relational databases (PostgreSQL, MariaDB, or MySQL). Strong collaboration and communication skills — able to partner with backend, frontend, and DevOps teams effectively. What We Offer You A leadership role at the helm of a dynamic and innovative cyber security SaaS company. The opportunity to shape the future of cyber security through cutting-edge technology and services. Competitive compensation package. A collaborative and supportive culture that values innovation, integrity, and inclusivity

title: Ingénieur en sécurité des applications senior (F/H)
description: Construisez la sécurité au cœur d’une transformation SaaS européenne Contexte & Missions Dans le cadre de sa croissance internationale, Orisha , éditeur européen de logiciels SaaS B2B, renforce ses capacités technologiques et poursuit la structuration de son organisation engineering. Pour accompagner cette dynamique, le groupe lance la création d’un hub technologique à Tunis , destiné à soutenir plusieurs Business Units à l’échelle européenne. Dans ce contexte, nous recherchons un Application Security Engineer pour jouer un rôle clé dans l’intégration de la sécurité au cœur des pratiques de développement du groupe. Ce poste n’est pas un rôle d’audit ou de conformité. Il s’agit d’un rôle hands-on, orienté build , visant à intégrer la sécurité dès les premières phases du cycle de développement ( approche “Shift Left” ) et à accompagner les équipes techniques dans la réduction des vulnérabilités à la source. Votre futur quotidien chez nous En tant qu’Application Security Engineer, vous interviendrez comme un partenaire clé des équipes de développement , avec un rôle transverse à l’échelle du groupe. Vos principales responsabilités : Intégrer la sécurité dans les pipelines CI/CD (approche DevSecOps) Déployer et maintenir des outils d’analyse de sécurité ( SAST, DAST, SCA ) Analyser et prioriser les vulnérabilités, en réduisant les faux positifs Accompagner les équipes de développement dans la correction des failles au niveau du code Exploiter les résultats des tests d’intrusion (pentests) Participer aux phases de conception produit ( threat modeling ) Définir et promouvoir des standards et bonnes pratiques de sécurité Animer une communauté de Security Champions Vous évoluerez dans un environnement multi-produits, international et orienté delivery , en collaboration étroite avec les équipes engineering, produit et sécurité du groupe. Et si c’était vous ? Profil recherché ✔ Vous avez un background solide en développement logiciel, DevSecOps ou sécurité applicative ✔ Vous êtes à l’aise dans des environnements CI/CD, cloud et automatisation ✔ Vous avez déjà travaillé avec des outils de sécurité applicative ( SAST, DAST, SCA ) ✔ Vous comprenez les enjeux de sécurisation du SDLC , au-delà de l’audit ✔ Vous aimez travailler au contact des développeurs et influencer les pratiques techniques ✔ Vous êtes pragmatique, orienté impact et à l’aise dans des environnements en structuration Langues ✔ Français courant ✔ Anglais courant Serait un plus ✔ Expérience avec des outils comme GitLab CI, Black Duck, Jira ✔ Connaissance des frameworks de sécurité type CIS Ce que vous gagnerez dans cette aventure Un rôle stratégique avec une forte visibilité à l’échelle du groupe L’opportunité de structurer les pratiques AppSec from scratch Une exposition à un environnement SaaS multi-produits international Un impact direct sur la qualité des produits et la posture sécurité globale Une culture basée sur la responsabilisation, la collaboration et l’excellence technique Pourquoi rejoindre Orisha ? Rejoindre Orisha, c’est : ✨ Participer à la construction d’un hub technologique stratégique en Tunisie ✨ Intégrer un groupe SaaS européen en forte croissance ✨ Contribuer à une transformation technologique ambitieuse ✨ Jouer un rôle clé dans la sécurisation de produits à fort impact Postulez dès maintenant Rejoignez-nous pour construire des plateformes SaaS sécurisées, scalables et modernes, au cœur de la transformation d’Orisha.

title: Operation Technologies & Compliance Specialist
description: SIT S.p.a., à travers les deux divisions Chauffage et Smart Gas Metering, a créé des solutions intelligentes pour contrôler les conditions environnementales et mesurer la consommation pour un monde plus durable. Multinational leader sur les marchés de référence et cotée sur le segment MTA de la Bourse italienne, SIT veut être le principal partenaire durable des solutions d'énergie et de climatisation au service des entreprises clientes, en accordant une grande attention à l'expérimentation et impact environnemental. Le Groupe est présent avec des sites de production en Italie, au Mexique, en Hollande, en Roumanie et en Chine, ainsi qu'une structure commerciale qui couvre tous les marchés mondiaux de référence. L'entreprise recherche la ressource suivante : Operation Technologies & Compliance Specialist Missions: Ce poste consiste à gérer et maintenir l'infrastructure informatique de l'usine. Le technicien assurera le bon fonctionnement des systèmes informatiques de production (serveurs, MES, postes de travail) et l'intégration des applications logicielles et des automates programmables, contribuant ainsi à l'efficacité des processus de production, aux mises à niveau des lignes de production pour répondre aux nouvelles exigences des clients et à la sécurité du système informatique. Gérer et maintenir la stabilité des serveurs et des systèmes informatiques. Configurer et assurer le support des PC de production et de post-production. Contrôler et superviser le système MES (Manufacturing Execution System). Assurer le support et la gestion de l'intégration des applications PC et PLC pour la traçabilité et le contrôle des processus. Élaborer les spécifications pour la mise en œuvre de nouvelles fonctions de contrôle et de traçabilité. Mettre en œuvre une politique de sécurité informatique pour protéger le périmètre de production. title: Privileged Access Management Engineer
description: We are seeking a skilled Privileged Access Management (PAM) Engineer to design, implement, and maintain our PAM solution. PAM Engineer needs to have a deep understanding of privileged access security principles and experience working with industry-leading PAM tools. This role will involve integrating PAM systems into our IT infrastructure, automating processes, and ensuring the security and compliance of privileged accounts across the organization. Key Responsibilities: PAM Implementation & Maintenance: Design, deploy, and configure PAM solutions such as Wallix, CyberArk, BeyondTrust. Integrate PAM systems with existing IT infrastructure, including Active Directory, LDAP, and cloud environments. Ensure the scalability, availability, and performance of PAM systems. Privileged Account Management: Automate privileged account provisioning, rotation, and de-provisioning processes. Manage and monitor privileged accounts, credentials, and access permissions across all systems. Security & Compliance: Enforce privileged access policies and ensure compliance with regulatory standards. Perform regular audits of privileged account activities and provide detailed reports. Monitor PAM logs for anomalies, unauthorized access, or potential security incidents. Required Skills: Expertise in deploying and managing PAM tools. Strong understanding of authentication protocols (e.g., Kerberos, SAML, OAuth) and directory services (e.g., Active Directory, LDAP) and Public Key Infrastructure Hands-on experience with OS: -Windows Server: RDS,NPS,Radius -Linux: Basic commands Scripting and automation skills (e.g., PowerShell or Bash) Knowledge of cybersecurity principles, including least privilege, zero trust, and privileged access controls. Required Profile: Formal Education: master’s degree in computer science, Cybersecurity, or related field. ● Professional Experience: Minimum of 2 to 5 years in a similar role. ● Communication: Effective communication skills, both oral and written. ● Analytical Ability: Exceptional problem-solving and analytical skills. ● Teamwork: Ability to collaborate effectively within a team. ● Language Proficiency: Fluent in technical English.

title: Team Lead, Engineering, Fintech
description: Who We Are Is What We Do. Deel is the all-in-one payroll and HR platform for global teams. Our vision is to unlock global opportunity for every person, team, and business. Built for the way the world works today, Deel combines HRIS, payroll, compliance, benefits, performance, and equipment management into one seamless platform. With AI-powered tools and a fully owned payroll infrastructure, Deel supports every worker type in 150+ countries—helping businesses scale smarter, faster, and more compliantly. Among the largest globally distributed companies in the world, our team of 7,000 spans more than 100 countries, speaks 74 languages, and brings a connected and dynamic culture that drives continuous learning and innovation for our customers. Why should you be part of our success story? As the fastest-growing Software as a Service (SaaS) company in history, Deel is transforming how global talent connects with world-class companies – breaking down borders that have traditionally limited both hiring and career opportunities. We're not just building software; we're creating the infrastructure for the future of work, enabling a more diverse and inclusive global economy. In 2024 alone, we paid $11.2 billion to workers in nearly 100 currencies and provided healthcare and benefits to workers in 109 countries—ensuring people get paid and protected, no matter where they are. Our momentum is reflected in our achievements and customer satisfaction: CNBC Disruptor 50, Forbes Cloud 100, Deloitte Fast 500, and repeated recognition on Y Combinator’s top companies list – all while maintaining a 4.83 average rating from 15,000 reviews across G2, Trustpilot, Captera, Apple and Google. Your experience at Deel will be a career accelerator. At the forefront of the global work revolution, you'll tackle complex challenges that impact millions of people's working lives. With our momentum—backed by a $17.3 billion valuation and $1 B in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) in just over five years—you'll drive meaningful impact while building expertise that makes you a sought-after leader in the transformation of global work. Team Lead About The Team At the core of Deel’s FinTech platform, responsible for building and operating client and contractor wallets across our regulated entities globally. This is a mission-critical domain where accuracy, compliance, and reliability are non-negotiable. Scope & Responsibilities Lead the fintech engineering team to design, build, and scale wallet infrastructure for global deel's users. Work with different teams to integrate wallets with regional banks through PSPs (via APIs), orchestrating pay-ins and ensuring seamless treasury operations. Depending on the project, you may collaborate with the Core Accounting team to connect wallets with the General Ledger (GL), ensuring balances are accurate and financial records meet audit standards. Work closely with Compliance, Treasury, and PSP Integration teams to ensure adherence to local regulations (MTL, PI, safeguarding requirements). Provide technical leadership in a global, fast-paced environment where uptime and precision are critical. Responsible for leading a high-performing engineering team, including running daily stand-ups, planning sessions, and backlog grooming. What We’re Looking For Location: Based in EMEA, with high availability for global collaboration. Background: Proven experience as a Team Lead or Manager in payments, banking, accounting, or treasury. FinTech Expertise: Hands-on wallets, flows of funds, compliance, and regulated money movement. Prior experience at companies like Wise, PayPal, or similar is a strong plus. Technical Skills: JavaScript/Node.js PostgreSQL with Sequelize ORM Strong understanding of distributed systems and API-driven banking/PSP integrations Leadership: Ability to run projects at scale, mentor engineers, and drive cross-functional alignment. Mindset: Ownership, detail-oriented, and mindful of the responsibility of managing other people’s money. Important note: This role will remain technically hands-on, with responsibilities divided between people management and hands-on tasks. Why This Role Matters This team is the foundation of Deel’s regulated money movement. The work ensures client funds are safe, compliant, and seamlessly accessible worldwide Total Rewards Our workforce deserves fair and competitive pay that meets them where they are. With scalable benefits, rewards, and perks, our total rewards programs reflect our commitment to inclusivity and access for all. Some things you’ll enjoy Stock grant opportunities dependent on your role, employment status and location Additional perks and benefits based on your employment status and country The flexibility of remote work, including optional WeWork access At Deel, we’re an equal-opportunity employer that values diversity and positively encourage applications from suitably qualified and eligible candidates regardless of race, religion, sex, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status, pregnancy or maternity or other applicable legally protected characteristics. Unless otherwise agreed, we will communicate with job applicants using Deel-specific emails, which include @ deel.com and other acquired company emails like @ payspace.com and @ paygroup.com . You can view the most up-to-date job listings at Deel by visiting our careers page . Deel is an equal-opportunity employer and is committed to cultivating a diverse and inclusive workplace that reflects different abilities, backgrounds, beliefs, experiences, identities and perspectives. Deel will provide accommodations on request throughout the recruitment, selection and assessment process for applicants with disabilities. If you require accommodations, please inform our Talent Acquisition Team via this link and a team member will be in touch to ensure your equal participation. If you have difficulty accessing the form, please email at recruiting@deel.com. As part of our hiring process, we primarily rely on interviews and role-related assessments. In limited cases, we may also consider informal background information relevant to the role, in line with our privacy and fairness obligations. This application process does utilise Automated Employment Decision Tools (AEDT) and AI systems to assist in evaluating candidates based on experience level, technical skills and qualifications. As a fully remote company, we also utilise AI-powered deepfake and fraud detection technologies to verify the authenticity of candidate identities and interactions during assessments and interviews. This processing is conducted in compliance with applicable Data Protection, AI Governance and Labour Laws. We ensure human oversight is maintained in all final hiring decisions. Your personal data is not used to train AI models. For more information on how we process your personal data, please see our Privacy Policy. For NYC Residents: In accordance with NYC Local Law 144, an independent bias audit has been conducted on AEDT; results are available at Ashby, Covey.

title: Architecte Data / Data Architect
description: Emploi Tunisie » Offres d'emploi à 1002 le Belvédère Tunis, Tunisie » Architecte Data / Data Architect Description de l'emploi Dans le cadre du développement de ses activités, Databiz filiale d'autobiz (groupe Stellantis) recherche un Architecte Data. Si vous êtes prêt à contribuer et à créer de la valeur pour la mobilité plus propre et plus intelligente de demain, Databiz est l'endroit où il faut être. Créée en 2004, AUTOBIZ est une société européenne, ayant aujourd’hui ses bureaux à Paris, Valence, Milan et Berlin. Elle compte aujourd’hui 200+ collaborateurs et fait partie des trois 1ers fournisseurs de valeurs de marché au monde du secteur automobile. Spécialisée dans la conception d’outils de données en temps réel, la société déploie, pour le compte de ses clients (constructeurs automobiles, concessionnaires, assureurs, loueurs longue durée) une large gamme de services/ solutions de pilotage et d’aide à la décision pour le secteur automobile Le groupe automobile PSA (Peugeot-Citroën) est entré au capital de l’entreprise en 2017. Cette collaboration renforce la position d’AUTOBIZ sur le marché automobile à l’international et lui permet de développer de nouvelles solutions au quotidien. Nous utilisons les données comme levier stratégique pour améliorer nos produits, anticiper les besoins de nos clients et prendre des décisions éclairées. Pour accompagner notre croissance et renforcer notre équipe Data, nous recrutons un(e) Architecte DATA passionné(e) par la performance, la fiabilité et la scalabilité des systèmes de données. Missions principales Concevoir, documenter et faire évoluer l’architecture data globale de l’entreprise : data lake, data warehouse, data mesh, modèles analytiques, flux temps réel, services orientés IA/ML, etc. Définir une architecture cible robuste, évolutive et alignée avec les cas d’usage business (BI, IA, automatisation, APIs, etc.). Encadrer et challenger les équipes data engineering, machine learning, MLOps et DevOps pour garantir la performance, la scalabilité, la sécurité et la maintenabilité des pipelines. Structurer la gouvernance des données (catalogue, lineage, qualité, sécurité, confidentialité, conformité RGPD). Accompagner les projets d’industrialisation IA/ML, notamment autour des LLM (Large Language Models), MLOps, Feature Store, monitoring des modèles. Participer activement à la roadmap data et IA/ML, en collaboration avec les équipes produit, métier et techniques. Piloter les choix technologiques stratégiques (outils d’orchestration, moteurs de calcul, bases de données, architectures hybrides). Définir et promouvoir les bonnes pratiques : modélisation, versionning, CI/CD, testabilité, observabilité des systèmes de données. Superviser les audits techniques des plateformes existantes et des nouveaux projets. Veiller à l’alignement avec les politiques de sécurité et d’architecture globales de l’entreprise. Compétences requises : Concepteur - développeur - base de données capable d'expliquer la donnée avec des schémas clairs. Maîtrise de la modélisation de données avancée (modèles conceptuels, logiques, physiques). Compréhension fine des changements d’état de la donnée (gestion des versions, états transitoires). Connaissances en infrastructure (serveurs, configuration, déploiement). Architecture & Technologies Expérience avérée en architecture data distribuée (batch & streaming). Maîtrise des composants GCP : BigQuery, Dataflow, Pub/Sub, Cloud Composer, Vertex AI, GCS, IAM, etc. Excellente connaissance des pipelines de données, ETL/ELT, orchestration (Airflow, Dagster, dbt…). Solide compréhension des architectures ML et MLOps : CI/CD pour modèles, monitoring, déploiement continu. Très bonne maîtrise des langages Python et Go, utilisés dans les développements de pipelines, microservices, et frameworks d’orchestration IA/ML. Maîtrise de Terraform et des principes d’Infrastructure as Code (IaC). Très bonne connaissance des modèles de données : en étoile, 3NF, Data Vault, Lakehouse, etc. Bonne maîtrise des outils de catalogue et gouvernance (ex: Data Catalog, Collibra, Alation…). Leadership & Collaboration : Aptitude à vulgariser les concepts techniques et à accompagner les équipes dans les choix d’architecture. Expérience de collaboration avec des équipes pluridisciplinaires : data scientists, ingénieurs, PO, sécurité, dev, ops. Capacité à piloter des audits techniques, participer aux comités d’architecture, et défendre les choix techniques au COMEX. Compétences appréciées (plus) : Expérience avec les LLM (Large Language Models), pipelines IA générative, fine-tuning et infrastructure associée. Connaissance des outils de test et d’observabilité : Great Expectations, Evidently AI, Prometheus, Grafana, etc. Connaissance des standards de sécurité dans la donnée et l’IA (audit trail, PII masking, data residency…). Certification GCP (Data Engineer ou Cloud Architect) ou équivalent Emploi Tunisie » Offres d'emploi à 1002 le Belvédère Tunis, Tunisie » Architecte Data / Data Architect

Solide compréhension des architectures ML et MLOps : CI/CD pour modèles, monitoring, déploiement continu. Très bonne maîtrise des langages Python et Go, utilisés dans les développements de pipelines, microservices, et frameworks d’orchestration IA/ML. Maîtrise de Terraform et des principes d’Infrastructure as Code (IaC). Créée en 2004, AUTOBIZ est une société européenne, ayant aujourd’hui ses bureaux à Paris, Valence, Milan et Berlin. Nous utilisons les données comme levier stratégique pour améliorer nos produits, anticiper les besoins de nos clients et prendre des décisions éclairées. Missions principales Concevoir, documenter et faire évoluer l’architecture data globale de l’entreprise : data lake, data warehouse, data mesh, modèles analytiques, flux temps réel, services orientés IA/ML, etc. Définir une architecture cible robuste, évolutive et alignée avec les cas d’usage business (BI, IA, automatisation, APIs, etc.). Définir et promouvoir les bonnes pratiques : modélisation, versionning, CI/CD, testabilité, observabilité des systèmes de données.
